Description: Research under the supervision of a faculty research adviser. Student must have research mentor approval prior to departmental consent. Department consent required. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for up to 6 units.

Subject: PR
Title: INDEPENDENT STUDY - HONORS
Description: Individualized approach to selected topics by guided reading and critical evaluation.  Department consent required. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for credit. Please complete the Independent Study Proposal form and obtain signatures of approval BEFORE registering.  https://nau.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/Independent_Study_Form_FINAL-3.pdf

Subject: SUS
Title: CMTY ENGM LOC TO GLB - HONORS
LongTitle: COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT FROM LOCAL TO GLOBAL - HONORS
Description: Focus on the relationships among local, regional, national, and transnational modes of political engagement in the context of national and transnational political and economic institutions. Explore crucial global challenges of our time such as human rights, war, democratic self-determination, poverty and inequality, climate change, food production, distribution, and security. Letter grade only.

Subject: CSD
Title: COM DVLPMNT IN CHILD - HONORS
Description: This course covers typical development of language and literacy from birth through later adolescence, with additional emphasis placed on cultural and linguistic differences. Letter grade only.

Subject: ACM
Title: MKT ARTS & CULTURE - HONORS
LongTitle: MARKETING AND MANAGEMENT OF ARTS AND CULTURAL ENTERPRISES - HONORS
Description: Marketing of arts and cultural enterprises in the non-profit, for profit, and public sector and management of marketing processes including research, planning, implementation, and evaluation. Letter grade only.

Subject: CSD
Title: SLPA PROF ISSUES - HONORS
Description: This course focuses on the ethical and professional issues fundamental to the role of the speech-language pathology assistant. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: FIELD WORK EXPERIENCE - HONORS
Description: Supervised field experience in an appropriate agency or organization. Pass/Fail only.  May be repeated as needed.

Subject: ES
Title: BLACK CINEMA - HONORS
Description: This course explores cinematic representations of African Americans and examinations of race in the United States. A range of topics will be explored including the depiction of Blacks in stereotypical ways, the construction of race, identity issues, group portrayals, interracial relationships, historical/cultural contexts, social position, intersection of race with gender and social class. Letter grade only.

Subject: POS
Title: CRITICAL RACE THEORY - HONORS
Description: Examines theoretical perspectives on race in the United States, with an emphasis on how race is defined and reproduced through politics and law. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENV
Title: SW ENV THRU TIME - HONORS
Description: An environmental and cultural history of the Southwest, from the time of the last glacial to the present. This class will cover aspects of both temporal scales and spatial scales. This class will cover many aspects of natural environmental history, placing the human societies in the context of environmental change. We will examine several types of scientific proxies that allow us to determine environmental history. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHI
Title: INDEPENDENT STUDY - HONORS
Description: Individualized approach to selected topics by guided reading and critical evaluation.  Department consent required. Pass/Fail only. No repeat limit. Please complete the Independent Study Proposal form and obtain signatures of approval BEFORE registering. https://nau.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/Independent_Study_Form_FINAL-3.pdf

Subject: MUP
Title: INSTR ENS UPPER DIV I - HONORS
LongTitle: INSTRUMENT ENSEMBLE, UPPER DIVISION I - HONORS
Description: Membership chosen by audition. Department consent required. Letter grade only. Two completions of each topic are allowed.

Subject: ENV
Title: ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY - HONORSP
Description: Political aspects of environmental problems, policy responses to those issues, and their impact on the human and natural environment. Letter grade only.

Subject: CMF
Title: UTV STUDIOS WORKSHOP - HONORS
Description: Hands-on experience in supervising staff members, overseeing facilities and resources, and managing creative media projects, film festivals, and other projects designated the faculty. For those with proven knowledge in producing, screenwriting, directing, cinematography, or editing, among other skills identified by the instructor useful for filmmaking and marketing. Requires instructor consent. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit.

Subject: SPA
Title: TOPICS IN SPAN-AM LIT - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (SPA 351 or SPA 352 or SPA 353) and Honors Student Group
Description: In-depth study of an important period, genre, or theme in Spanish-American literature.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: SPA
Title: PENINSULAR SPAN LIT - HONORS
LongTitle: TOPICS IN PENINSULAR SPANISH LITERATURE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (SPA 351 or SPA 352 or SPA 353) and Honors Student Group
Description: In-depth study of an important period, genre, or theme in peninsular Spanish literature.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: PHO
Title: FOOD AND DECOR PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Studio course. Photography of food and decor for strategic use in advertisements, packaging, menus and cookbooks using natural light and lighting systems. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: STUDIO & FIELD PHOTO TOPICS
Description: Theory, training and experience in a specific area of photography. Digital-SLR required. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 9 units with different topics.

Subject: PHO
Title: ADVENTURE PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Photography of travel, extreme sports and daring feats using natural light and lighting systems. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: EVENT PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Photography of social functions using natural light and lighting systems. Emphasizes weddings, concerts and sports. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: LOCATION PORTRAITURE
Description: Photography of human subjects on location using natural light and lighting systems.  Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: NATURE PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Photography of landscapes and wildlife using natural light and lighting systems.  Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: STUDIO B&W (DARKROOM) PHOTO
Description: In this course students will use film cameras and the darkroom to construct black and white images in studio lighting environments. Adjustable camera required. Lab-studio arranged. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: STUDIO PRODUCT PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Photography of products in studio using lighting systems. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: BUSINESS OF PHOTOGRPHY
Description: Introduction to branding, marketing, copyright law, entrepreneurial practices, financial management, forms, contracts, and other practical matters related to the business of photography. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: SCIENTIFIC PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Photography of subjects that are too small, too far, too fast, and too difficult to see with the naked eye, using natural light and lighting systems. Emphasizes aesthetics of time lapse, aerial, high-speed, forensic, micrography and astrophotography. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash and a wide aperture lens (minimum f2.8) required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: ADV PHOTOGRAPHY WORKFLOW
Description: Advanced image manipulation and printing techniques. Emphasizes third party plugins and delivery for exhibition and publication.  Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: STUDIO PORTRAITURE
Description: Photography of human subjects in studio using natural light and lighting systems.   Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: LOCATION B&W (DIGITAL) PHOTO
Description: Monochromatic photography on-location using natural light and lighting systems.  Emphasizes landscapes, architecture, and street photography. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: NEGOTIATION & CONFLICT MGMT
Description: Introduces theoretical and practical applications of negotiation across multiple contexts.  Students engage in several simulated negotiations in order to understand how to craft a conflict resolution plan and then improvise as needed.  Special attention will be paid to salary negotiation and negotiation via modalities such as email and text messaging.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: LEADERSHIP
Description: Effective leadership; traits, behaviors, including power and influence; effect of situations and contingencies; leader styles; transformational and charismatic leadership; socio-cultural issues; and ethical behavior.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CSD
Title: CLINICAL METHODS LAB
LongTitle: CLINICAL AND EDUCATION METHODS IN SPEECH-LANGUAGE PATHOLOGY LAB
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  Admission to the CSD Graduate program  
Corequisite:  CSD 510
Description: This course is designed to provide students an opportunity to develop the foundational skills needed by graduate clinicians, prior to starting their first clinical practicum. Students enrolled in CSD 510L will gain familiarity with the processes and procedures for effective clinical management of individuals with communication disorders, including planning and implementing treatment, collecting data and documenting progress, and completing clinical paperwork. Letter grade only.

Subject: AS
Title: AEROSPACE STUDIES LDRSHP LAB
Description: Application of leadership and management theories and concerns through participation in advanced leadership experiences; weight and fitness training.  Per 10 U.S.C.2109(c)(1), Practical Military Training, "A person who is not qualified for, and...will not be able to become qualified for, advanced training (POC),...shall not be permitted to participate in...practical military training," including LLAB. Pass/Fair only.

Subject: HS
Title: FIELDWORK EXPERIENCE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (Public Health Milestone or Health Sciences: Allied Health) and Honors Student Group
Description: Supervised fieldwork experience in a professional health setting.  Instructor consent required.  Pass-fail only.  May be repeated for a maximum of 9 units.

Subject: ENT
Title: MGT & MKT IN NON-PROFIT ENTRE
LongTitle: MARKETING AND MANAGEMENT IN NON-PROFIT AND SOCIAL ENTREPRENEURSHIP
Description: Study of marketing and management concepts and practices as they relate to non-profit and social enterprises.  The role of non-profits and social entrepreneurship; developing an effective marketing strategy and managing for results in non-profit and social enterprises.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ME
Title: FLUID MECHANICS II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CS 122 and ME 395 with grades of C or better and ME Fdtnl GPA Milestone for ME majors) or (CS 122 and ME 395 with grades of C or better for non-ME majors)
Description: Applications of the governing differential and integral equations of fluid mechanics to fundamental problems in internal flows, pipe flow, external flows, turbulent and laminar flows, boundary layers, lift and drag. Also presented are applications of dimensional analysis, and introductions to compressible flow, fluid machinery, and computational fluid dynamics. Principles are reinforced with laboratory experiments. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: EBP PLANNING IMPLEMENTATION
LongTitle: EVIDENCE-BASED PRACTICE:  PROJECT PLANNING AND IMPLEMENTATION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to DNP program, NUR 609, NUR 677
Description: This course focuses on identifying methods, tools or measurements, and outcomes for the execution of the DNP Scholarly Project.  At the conclusion of this course, students will be prepared for their scholarly proposal oral defense.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ENV
Title: CONSERVATION BIOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (BIO 226, ENV 226, ENV 326, or FOR 313); and Honors student group
Description: Scientific basis for conserving and managing biological diversity, emphasizing applications of ecology, drawing on other scientific disciplines, and integrating public policy and sociological issues. Letter grade only.

Subject: BA
Title: INDIVIDUALS, TEAMS, & CAREERS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Business Administration (MBA) or Healthcare, Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Description: Introduces human-asset issues in organizations from personal, legal, and management perspectives.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ACC
Title: FIN RPRTNG & STMT ANALYSIS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Business Administration (MBA) or Healthcare, Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Description: Firm valuation models; accounting theory pertaining to financial reporting alternatives and issues; analysis of financial reporting pertaining to distress prediction, forecasting earnings and cash flows, and equity analysis.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BA
Title: STRATEGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Business Administration (MBA) or Healthcare, Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Description: Students evaluate strategic business challenges, considering managerial implications of organizational issues, to make high-level decisions and determine proper leadership to implement those decisions. Students use analytical tools and class discussion to formulate and implement strategy. Letter grade only.

Subject: MKT
Title: CONTEMPORARY DEVELOPMENTS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Business Administration (MBA) or Healthcare, Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Description: Examines recent trends and investigations in a selected area of a particular field of study.  May be offered no more than three times before being submitted for a permanent course number.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for credit.

Subject: BIO
Title: ECOLOGY LAB - HONORS
Prerequisites: Pre- or Corequisite: BIO 226H and Honors Student Group
Description: Experimental analysis of plant and animal interactions within ecosystems, including community dynamics, ecological processes, and biotic inventories. Letter grade only.

Subject: TH
Title: ADVANCED CONTEMPORARY ACTING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: TH 332, Admission to a Theatre plan (BA, BS, or Minor)
Description: Synthesizes internal process and external technique.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CMF
Title: MEDIA DEVELOPMENT WRITING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement; (CMF 121 and one from (CMF 221, CMF 235, or CMF 275) with grades of C or better)); and Junior Status or higher
Description: Writing skills and techniques for use in the entertainment industry.  Coverage, pitching, project proposals, and other genres of writing included. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: WRTNG IN PSYCH SCI
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: PSY 202,  PSY 230, and Foundation English Requirement
Pre- or Corequisite: (PSY 290 or PSY 302W)
All prerequisites must have grades of C or better
Description: This course introduces writing techniques used in psychological research such as effectively integrating logical reasoning and empirical evidence and opportunities for career preparation and professional development. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: ECO
Title: INT MICROECON THEORY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ECO 201 or equivalent, ECO 284, ECO 285, Junior Status, and Honors Student Group
Description: Nature of economic analysis; theories of demand, production, and supply; price and output determination; and resource utilization, and market failure. Letter grade only.

Subject: HUM
Title: TPCS 19 C TO PRESENT - HONORS
LongTitle: TOPICS THE 19TH CENTURY TO THE PRESENT - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((Sophomore Status or higher), or admission to any 90-30 plan); and Honors Student Group
Description: Selected studies of major works and styles in the arts and literature from the 19th century to the present. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: HUM
Title: HOLOCAUST - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((Sophomore Status or higher), or admission to any 90-30 plan); and Honors Student Group
Description: The Holocaust is put in cultural, historical and ethical perspectives with reflections on its meaning and impact on contemporary society. Includes study of anti-Judaism, modern antisemitism, racial ideology, and Nazi genocidal policy. Ghettoization, camps, strategies of victimization, and survival are examined. Students also study bystanders, perpetrators, victims, Allied responses, as well as issues of historiography and moral accountability.  Letter grade only.

Subject: HUM
Title: TOPICS MED ART & CULT - HONORS
LongTitle: TOPICS IN MEDIEVAL ART AND CULTURE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((Sophomore Status or higher), or admission to any 90-30 plan); and Honors Student Group
Description: Selected studies of major works and styles in the arts and literature from antiquity to the Renaissance for their humanistic perspectives. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: HUM
Title: STUDIES GLOBAL CLTRS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((Sophomore Status or higher), or admission to any 90-30 plan); and Honors Student Group
Description: Examines the arts and literature of selected peoples for their humanistic ideas and values. Alternate semesters focus on different areas of the world. Letter grade or Pass/Fail. May be repeated for up to 6 hours of credit in another focus.

Subject: HUM
Title: CULTURE & TECHNOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((Sophomore Status or higher), or admission to any 90-30 plan); and Honors Student Group
Description: Examines the meaning and role of humanistic values (e.g., tolerance and freedom) in relation to our technological society. Letter grade only.

Subject: ADV
Title: ADVER COPY AND LAYOUT - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (STR 207, COM 101, and Foundation English Requirement with grades of C or better); and Honors Student Group
Description: Practical application of advertising design techniques and electronic layout. Creative strategy. Intensive practice in copy writing for various media channels. Taught in a computer lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: MUS
Title: EAR TRAINING I
Description: How to conceptualize written diatonic melodies before hearing them and transcribe the same into notation after hearing, and improvise with these materials. Note that MUS 131 is normally taken concurrently with MUS 121. Letter grade only.

Subject: CENE
Title: WATER QUALITY ANALYSIS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 137 and CHM 151 with grades of C or better
Description: The chemical, physical, and biological processes that control water quality in lakes and streams, and the systems of differential equations that can be used to describe these transformations. Derivation and application of these equations using spreadsheets and prepackaged software. Procedures for calibrating and verifying these models and analysis of the capabilities and limitations of mathematical representations of natural systems. Co-convenes with CENE 562. Letter grade only.

Subject: TH
Title: VOICE AND MOVEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to Theatre (BA), Theatre (BS) or Theatre (MINOR)
Description: Development of naturalness and flexibility in the body and in the speaking voice; techniques for refining the actors instrument and creating character through the synthesis of movement and speech. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: SNSRY & QLTY ASPCTS BEER LAB
LongTitle: SENSORY AND QUALITY ASPECTS OF BEER LAB
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Students must be 21+ years of age on the first day of class
Pre- or Corequisite: CHM 312
Description: This lab will complement the Sensory and Quality Analysis of Beer course with practical experience in a brewing microbiological and chemical analysis lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: UNIV PHYSICS I FOR PHYSICISTS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Astronomy BS, Physics & Astrophysics BS, Physics BS, Secondary Education-Physics BSEd, or Informatics BS
Pre- or Corequisite: MAT 136
Description: A calculus-based introductory physics course designed for physics majors. Classical Mechanics, Gravitation, Fluids. Credit will not be given for both PHY 161 and PHY 171. 4 hours lecture, 3 hours lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: ENTOMOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: BIO 181, BIO 182, and Honors Student Group
Corequisite: BIO 322L
Description: Biology, anatomy, physiology, ecology, and evolution of insects. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: ORNITHOLOGY LAB
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: (BIO 226, ENV 326, or FOR 213), and Junior Status or higher
Pre- or Corequisite: BIO 427
Description: Evolution, ecology, behavior, and conservation of birds. Letter grade only.

Subject: INF
Title: RESEARCH INITIATION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Informatics BS program; Junior status or higher
Description: A structured introduction to interdisciplinary research in informatics and one of the Bachelors of Science in Informatics (BSI) emphasis areas. Topics include research and lab techniques, experimental design, and team work in a lab context. Letter grade only.

Subject: BAN
Title: ENTERPRISE DATABASE CONCEPTS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (ISM 220 with a grade of C or better); and (Business Professional Program Milestone or Business Analytics Milestone)
Description: In-depth investigation and application of database concepts including:  data models, application development using host language and DBMS development tools, and data warehousing. Co-convened with BAN 510. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHA
Title: HC SYS & PRACT U. S.
LongTitle: HEALTH CARE SYSTEMS AND PRACTICES IN THE U. S.
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to DMSc program, Physician Assistant Studies-Health Equity GCRT, or Physician Assistant Studies-Operations and Leadership GCRT
Description: This course provides students the opportunity to think creatively and critically about how health and health care are delivered in the U.S. The course provides an in-depth analysis of the complex organization and structure of the U.S. health care system; the various entities involved in the delivery of care in the U.S. and an understanding of how these factors affect health care health care performance and outcomes. Letter grade only

Subject: AS
Title: AEROSPACE STUDIES LDRSHP LAB
Description: Continuation of AS 401L.  Per 10 U.S.C.2109(c)(1), Practical Military Training, "A person who is not qualified for, and...will not be able to become qualified for, advanced training (POC),...shall not be permitted to participate in...practical military training," including LLAB. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for up to 3 units of credit.

Subject: EDU
Title: TEACHER AID PRACTICUM
Description: Field experience in an elementary or secondary school classroom; exposure to and practical experiences in the tasks of a classroom teacher.  For elementary majors, this field experience is shared by numerous methods courses across several semesters.  Available only at NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Pass-fail only.  May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit.

Subject: EDU
Title: SCIENCE IN THE ELEM SCHOOL
Description: Objectives, content, and methodology of elementary science; selection and use of texts, equipment, and supplementary materials.  This course contains an assessment that must be successfully completed in order to register for student teaching.  Available only at NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDU
Title: SOCIAL STUDIES IN THE ELEM SCH
LongTitle: SOCIAL STUDIES IN THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL
Description: Role, objectives, and content of the social studies curriculum as well as methodology, selection of materials, and evaluation of social studies learning.  Available only at NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDU
Title: INTEGRATED LITERACY II
LongTitle: INTEGRATED LITERACY II: READING THEORY, DECODING, AND LANGUAGE ARTS IN THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL
Description: Language and literacy development, from kindergarten through eighth grade.  Supports childrens reading, writing, and language arts pedagogy with current and classic research.  5 hrs. lecture, 1 hr. lab.  Available only at NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDU
Title: EVALUATION OF LEARNING: ELEM
Description: Principles and techniques of evaluation of achievement in education at the elementary level.  This course contains an assessment that must be successfully completed in order to register for student teaching.  Available only at the NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDU
Title: INTEGRATED LITERACY I
LongTitle: INTEGRATED LITERACY I: DEVELOPMENTAL LITERACY AND LANGUAGE ARTS IN THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL
Description: Language acquisition and emergent literacy from birth through third grade. Examines contexts that support childrens language and literacy development.  Available only at the NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDU
Title: ELEMENTARY SCHOOL CURRICULUM
Description: Content, organization, methods, and materials for grades K-8. Lab and field experience.  This course contains an assessment that must be successfully completed in order to register for student teaching.  Available only at NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDU
Title: MATH IN THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL
Description: Teaching of mathematics in the elementary and junior high school; opportunity for the development of instructional materials.  Available only at the NAU-Yuma Branch campus.  Letter grade only.

Subject: TSM
Title: CLASSROOM INTERACTIONS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (TSM 201 and (TSM 301 or TSM 303W)) with grades of C or better
Description: This course is an exploration of the role content and pedagogy play in creating an equitable and responsive learning environment. This course integrates theory and practice in ways appropriate for secondary mathematics and science classrooms by focusing on interactions between teachers, students, and content. Diversity, equity, and classroom learning opportunities will be discussed in relationship to creating a safe and productive learning environment for all students. Students should be prepared for practicum expectations. Practicum may occur throughout the semester during school hours. Students will need to have time in their schedule, outside of course time, to complete practicum expectations. Letter grade only.

Subject: CTE
Title: MTHDS OF CAREER & TECHNICAL ED
LongTitle: METHODS OF CAREER AND TECHNICAL EDUCATION
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  Junior status or higher or CTE Certificate  
Pre- or Corequisite:  CTE 300
Description: Examines philosophy and teaching methods, preparation and use of instructional material, and evaluation in agriculture education, business/marketing education, family and consumer education, health careers education, industrial technological education, and education. Satisfies methods course requirement for ADE CTE certification. Co-convened with CTE 565. Letter grade only.

Subject: MGBA
Title: STRTGC OP & SPPLY CHAIN MGMT
LongTitle: STRATEGIC OPERATIONS AND SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to MGBA program and MGBA 500
Description: Covers the operations function with a strategic supply chain focus, emphasizing the need for integration between operations and the other business functions and between the firm and its global supply chain partners.  Topics include product and process design, planning, sourcing, inventory, capacity, quality, production, logistics, customer service and sustainable operations.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MGT
Title: HUMAN RESOURCES MGMT - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((MGT 300 or MGT 300I) and Business Professional Program) or (MGT 303 and Psych Human Resources UCERT) and Honors Student Group
Description: A study of the functions of human resource organizations.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ME
Title: MECHANICAL ENGR DESIGN II
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: (Mechanical Egr or Mech & Robot Egr BS); and ME 476C with a grade of C or better
Mechanical Egr majors must have ME Fdtnl GPA Milestone
Description: Design methodology and decision making. Team design projects culminating with oral and written reports. Individual projects are appropriate on approval. Letter grade only.

Subject: ME
Title: ADVANCED CAD/CAM
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (ME 180, CS 122 and MAT 137 with grades of C or better and ME Fdtnl GPA Milestone for ME majors) or  (ME 180, CS 122 and MAT 137 with grades of C or better for non-ME majors)
Description: Three-dimensional CAD, parametric feature-based design, computer-integrated manufacturing, computer rendering and animation, team design project. Letter grade only.

Subject: NTS
Title: CURR TOPICS IN NUTR & DIETETIC
LongTitle: CURRENT TOPICS IN NUTRITION AND DIETETICS
Description: This course covers contemporary topics within the field of dietetics, such as informatics, integrative and functional medicine, ethics, cultural competence, sustainable practices, and international nutrition. Students will gain the necessary skills to current and future clients with the use of technology and sustainable food practices while considering the needs of culturally diverse audiences. Topics such as nutrigenetics and integrative and functional medicine practices are the wave of the future in dietetics practice and students will be prepared for their careers, armed with the latest evidence and practices in the field. Letter grade only.

Subject: POS
Title: UNDERGRADUATE RES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: POS 100, POS 301W, POS 304, 75 earned units, and Honors Student Group
Description: Original research under the supervision of a research adviser. Department consent required. Letter grade or Pass/Fail. May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit.

Subject: BBA
Title: SUPPLY CHAIN & OPERATIONS MGMT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Full Admission to the BBA or LSCM Certificate); or ((LSCM or Industrial Tech Management majors) and (BBA 290, BBA 291, and BBA 292 with grades of C or better))
Description: Covers management of operations, the activities utilized to produce the products and/or services sold by the firm.  Emphasizes process design and improvement, including the inter-firm processes that manage the twin flows of inventory and information between supply chain members.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: OPERATING SYSTEMS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CS 249 or CYB 136) with a grade of C or better
Description: Theory behind operating systems; scheduling, memory management, I/O and concurrency. Letter grade only.

Subject: AS
Title: NAT SEC & PREP ACTV DUTY II
LongTitle: NATIONAL SECURITY AND PREPARATION FOR ACTIVE-DUTY
Description: Continuation of AS 401. This course is designed for college seniors and provides them the foundation to understand their role as military officers and how they are directly tied to our National Security Strategy. It is an overview of the complex social and political issues facing the military profession and requires a measure of sophistication commensurate with the senior college level. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: PHYSICAL CHEMISTRY II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CHM 341 and Pre- or Corequisite: MAT 238
Description: Introduces quantum and statistical mechanics, group theory, and spectroscopy.  Coconvenes with CHM 540.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: INFO AND TECHNOLOGY LITERACY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 110 or higher) and Foundation English Requirement
Description: Study of the pervasive influence of information and technology systems in organizations and society.  Acquire and enhance skills to organize and apply information using databases, spreadsheets, word and information processing, and presentation graphics; evaluate the effectiveness and reliability of various information sources for their appropriate use.  Critical evaluation of public policy issues relating to computers and information technology.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: ECOLOGY LAB
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement
Corequisite: BSC 326
Description: Scientific analysis of plant and animal interactions within ecosystems, including community dynamics, ecological processes, and biotic inventories in the natural environment. In addition to specific skills, this course will emphasize effective writing, an essential skill in the biological sciences. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: SPA
Title: SPANISH APPLIED LING - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (SPA 304W or SPA 312W) and Honors Student Group
Description: Focuses on the relationship between Spanish linguistic systems and instructional practices.  Provides an understanding of Spanishs linguistic components and variation.  Students utilize this knowledge to analyze instructional and curricular practices.  Letter grade only.

Subject: OTD
Title: FIELDWORK LEVEL II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  OTD 701 (6 units), OTD 748, OTD 762, OTD 772 and Cumulative GPA 3.0
Description: The goal of Level II fieldwork is to develop as a competent, entry-level, generalist occupational therapist. This fieldwork is a 24 week, full time, in-depth experience in delivering occupational therapy services to clients through engaging purposeful and meaningful occupation, research, and administrative activities. The student will see a variety of clients across the lifespan in a variety of settings. Students must have completed all health and background requirements. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for up to 24 units of  credit.

Subject: ENV
Title: SUSTAINABLE ECOLOGY
Description: In this millennium the applications of ecology require a thorough understanding and use of sustainability concepts. Applied Ecology is designed for environmental studies students with the objectives of understanding ecosystems, ecological communities and population change through the lenses of sustainability. These include biodiversity, natural capital, resilience and mitigation. Students may not receive credit for both ENV 324 and ENV 326. Letter grade only.

Subject: HIS
Title: PUBLIC HISTORY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any course that satisfies NAU's Junior Level Writing Requirement (JLWR)
Description: Public History is defined as the employment of historians and the historical method outside of academia: in government, private corporations, the media, historical societies and museums, even in private practice. As a senior-level seminar, Public History prepares students for internships and careers in history jobs outside the classroom including government, archives, historical societies, museums, historic preservation, local history, cultural resource management, and a variety of other fields. This course is divided equally between an intensive reading colloquium covering the first eight weeks of the course and a hands-on, collaborative, class-based public history project for the remainder of the class. Co-convened with HIS 502. Letter grade only.

Subject: TH
Title: DRAFTING FOR THEATRE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to a Theatre plan (BA, BS, or Minor)
Description: Techniques of hand and CAD drafting for the theatre. Letter grade only.

Subject: TH
Title: DRAWING & RENDERING THEATRE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to a Theatre plan (BA, BS, or Minor)
Description: Techniques of drawing and rendering for the theatre design and technology student.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CST
Title: SENIOR SEMINAR IN COMM STUDIES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CST 271, CST 300W, 15 hours of CST coursework, and Senior Status
Description: Surveys recent research and conceptual trends in communication studies. During the course, you prepare a substantive research project and publicly present and defend your conclusions. Letter grade only.

Subject: FW
Title: HEALTH COACHING SKILLS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: PSY 101, HS 200, and NTS 135 with grades of C or better in each
Description: This course will provide health coaching skills for allied health, health promotion, and public health professionals with emphasis on the practical application of brief intervention and motivational interviewing skills to promote healthy lifestyle changes.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ME
Title: EXPMTL MTHDS THERMAL SCIENCES
LongTitle: EXPERIMENTAL METHODS IN THE THERMAL SCIENCES
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: CENE 225, EE 188, and ME 395 
Pre- or Corequisite: ME 450
All prerequisites must have grades of C or better
ME majors must also have ME Fdtnl GPA Milestone
Description: Fundamental methods of acquiring and analyzing experimental data in the thermal sciences, including applied statistics. Experiments in thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, heat transfer, and thermal-fluid systems. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: FNDNS PHYSIOLOGY LAB - HONORS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Honors Student Group
Pre- or Corequisite: BIO 325H
Description: Lab experience in the study of animal tissues, organs, and organ systems in the maintenance of homeostasis in animals and humans. Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: INTRO STATISTICS IN PSYCHOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: PSY 101 and Math Foundation Requirement with grades of C or better
Description: Basic concepts of statistical analysis and design in psychological research. Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: HOSPITALITY MARKETING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Hotel & Restaurant (BS), or Parks & Recreation (BS), or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Marketing objectives and strategies related to consumer demands, marketing planning, and selling methodologies for the hospitality industry. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: ANALYTICAL CHEMISTRY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CHM 152 or CHM 230), Honors Student Group
Description: Principles and practices of chemical analysis with an emphasis on stoichiometry and equilibrium calculations. Recommended: CHM 320L. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: LAB SAFETY AND SUPERVISION
Description: Rigorous introduction to lab safety, use and disposal of hazardous chemicals, emergency procedures, toxicology, and lab supervision.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 4 units of credit.

Subject: CHM
Title: FUNDAMENTAL CHEMISTRY RECITATN
Description: Optional recitation section covering problem-solving techniques in fundamental general chemistry.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: ENG
Title: RSCH ISSUES IN ENG LANG TEACHG
LongTitle: SEMINAR IN RESEARCH ISSUES IN ENGLISH LANGUAGE TEACHING
Description: Examines latest research and practice in ESL methodology and materials construction, administration, testing, computer-aided instruction, and curricula. Letter grade only. May be repeated for credit.

Subject: ESE
Title: F & M SPED: MOD TO SEVERE DIS
LongTitle: FOUNDATIONS AND METHODS OF SPECIAL EDUCATION: MODERATE TO SEVERE DISABILITIES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ESE 280, and admission to Professional Education Program (SPED Milestone)
Description: This course covers the IDEA Definitions for students who have moderate to severe disabilities, including characteristics and etiologies, and legal and educational provisions. It also reviews cognitive, social, and language development of individuals who experience moderate to severe disabilities. In addition to foundational elements related to moderate to severe disabilities, the course also covers evidence-based methods and strategies for teaching students who have moderate to severe disabilities. Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: FOUNDATIONS & METHODS CLDE
LongTitle: FOUNDATIONS AND METHODS FOR CULTURALLY AND LINGUISTICALLY DIVERSE STUDENTS WITH EXCEPTIONALITIES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ESE 280, and admission to Professional Education Program (SPED Milestone)
Description: Foundations, legal aspects, and educational interventions related to culturally and linguistically diverse students with special education needs.  Emphasis on the importance of native culture and first and second language acquisition.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MUP
Title: KEYBOARD HARMONY I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MUP 102 with a grade of C or better, or permission through successful completion of a placement exam.
Description: Continued development of skills learned in MUP 102. Emphasis on music reading, keyboard theory and technique, solo/ensemble repertoire, instrumental/choral score reading, accompanying, and creative activities within a group setting. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: TOPOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Mathematics MS or (MAT 411 and MAT 531 with grades of C or better)
Description: Topological spaces, metric spaces, homeomorphism, compactness, connectedness, separation axioms, product and quotient spaces, local properties, elementary algebraic, and geometric topology. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: NUCLEAR PHYSICS
Description: Nuclear decay, nuclear stability, structure of nuclei, gamma radiation, alpha and beta decay, fission, radiation techniques.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: CALCULUS II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 136 with a grade of C or better
Description: Concepts, techniques, and applications of integration, differential equations, Taylor polynomials, infinite series. For the Liberal Studies Program, this course will fulfill either the Mathematics foundation requirement or a Science Applied Science distribution block requirement. If you have already fulfilled your Mathematics foundation requirement, this course will fulfill a Science Applied Science distribution block requirement. For the General Studies Program, this course will fulfill either the Mathematics foundation requirement or a Natural Science distribution block requirement. If you have already fulfilled your Mathematics foundation requirement, this course will fulfill a Natural Science distribution block requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: INTRO MATHEMATICAL REASONING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 136 with a grade of C or better
Description: Mathematical reasoning in multi-step problems across different areas of mathematics.  Focuses on problem solving and solution writing.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: EVALUATION OF LEARNING: ELEM
Description: This course will help students to develop the knowledge, skills, and dispositions necessary to effectively assess, evaluate and monitor student learning and growth in grades K-8 in a professionally responsible and ethical manner. These include understanding data literacy as a guide to decision making; examining a variety of formal and informal assessments; evaluating their relative merits and limitations; understanding the concepts of validity and reliability; and formulating an initial assessment philosophy that recognizes the social and political milieu of schooling as well as classroom students with exceptionalities. Letter grade only.

Subject: GER
Title: SECOND YEAR GERMAN - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (GER 102 or GER 105) and Honors Student Group
Description: Continuation of GER 101 and 102. Letter grade only.

Subject: ART
Title: SCULPTURE III
Description: Methods of Fabricated Sculpture - Studio Course. Explores various assembly methods for sculpture in fabric, wood, welded metals, plastic and a variety of other materials. Letter grade onlh. May be repeated for a maximum of 6 units.

Subject: PHY
Title: MECHANICS I
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  PHY 263 and (PHY 265 or CS 122)
Pre- or Corequisite:  MAT 239 or higher
Description: Review of Newtonian mechanics, drag forces, computer simulation of dynamical systems, systems of particles, oscillators, non-inertial frames of reference, Lagrangian mechanics, orbital dynamics.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: MULTIVARIATE STATISTICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to Psychology (MA) and PSY 625
Description: Application of multivariate techniques to research. Undergraduate course in statistics required before taking PSY 626. Letter grade only.

Subject: ID
Title: SENIOR PROJECT
Description: Studio course. Development of major design problem under the direction of a faculty member. Drawings, models, details, oral presentation for jury consideration. Project is exhibited and a grade determined by the committee. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: CENE
Title: MECHANICS OF MATERIALS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  CENE 251 with a grade of C or better
Description: Calculation of axial, flexural, torsional stresses and deformations in components such as beams, shafts, connections and columns. Characterization of material properties including elastic modulus, shear modulus, thermal coefficients, and other common engineering properties. Letter grade only.

Subject: CENE
Title: ENVIRONMENTAL BIOTECHNOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  CENE 280 and (CHM 230 or CHM 235) with grades of C or better
Description: Environmental Biotechnology (EB) is a wide ranging field that aims to use living organisms in the environment to provide services to society, including remediation, water/soil/solid/air treatment, production of clean energies, among many others. In this course you will learn the fundamentals of EB, how to apply them in engineering, and how to use biology and microbiology tools to design and assess environmental engineering systems. Additionally, the course will provide a space to acquire knowledge through literature review and give students tools for better writing. Letter grade only.

Subject: POS
Title: LEGISLATIVE INTERNSHP - HONORS
LongTitle: LEGISLATIVE OR THE WASHINGTON CENTER INTERNSHIP - HONORS
Description: Supervised internship in the Arizona Legislature or the Washington Center. Students are selected on a competitive basis, and you must apply the term prior to the internship. Students must meet requirements of the program to be eligible. Course enrollment is handled by the internship coordinator after selections have been made. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: BIO
Title: INTRO HUMAN MVT - HONORS
Description: Overview of the biological basis of human movement and function, including health, disease, and human performance. Provides information on the subject matter, career opportunities, and current literature in exercise physiology. Letter grade only.

Subject: MUP
Title: LESSONS & SR RECITAL - HONORS
LongTitle: APPLIED LESSONS AND SENIOR RECITAL - HONORS
Description: Preparing music and written background information for public performance. Department consent required. Letter grade only.

Subject: EE
Title: CONTEMP DEVELOPMENTS - HONORS
Description: Examines recent trends and investigations in a selected area of a particular field of study. May be offered no more than three times before being submitted for a permanent course number. Letter grade only. No repeat limit.

Subject: HIS
Title: MODERN CHINA - HONORS
Description: Examines the political, socioeconomic, and cultural-intellectual developments in China from the 1830s to the 1940s.  Focuses on the dual processes of modernization and revolution.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PHI
Title: METAPHYSICS - HONORS
Description: Issues include space and time, causality, contingency and necessity, form and matter, cause and substance, reason, language, and being.  Letter grade only. Recommended: three units of philosophy (PHI) coursework, or instructors consent.

Subject: REL
Title: JUD - CHR SCRPTR(OT) - HONORS
Description: Examines the major themes and ideas of the books of the Bible shared by the Jewish and Christian religions (i.e., Jewish Tanakh, Christian Old Testament) in the historical context of their origin. Letter grade only.

Subject: WGS
Title: MASCULINITIES IN U.S. - HONORS
LongTitle: MAKING "REAL MEN": MASCULINITIES IN THE UNITED STATES - HONORS
Description: Examines how race, gender, and sexualities influence the diverse experiences of boys/men in the U.S., focusing on discourses of masculinity, socialization of boys/men, boys/mens actions and perceptions of themselves, other boys/men, women, and social realities. Letter grade only.

Subject: HON
Title: HONORS SEMINAR: SCI LITERCY
LongTitle: HONORS SEMINAR: SCIENTIFIC LITERACY - NATURAL SCIENCES: SAS
Description: Honors seminar that carries Scientific Literacy - Natural Sciences General Studies credit. Topics will vary by semester. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: ENV
Title: UNDERGRAD RESEARCH - HONORS
Description: Original research under the supervision of a research adviser.  Department consent required. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit.

Subject: SA
Title: STUDY ABROAD - AHI-GD - HONORS
LongTitle: TOPICS IN STUDY ABROAD - AHI-GD - HONORS
Description: Only available to Study Abroad students while participating in a Study Abroad program.  Content includes courses that enhance understanding of different cultures of the world through the study of language, literature, religion, and artistic creations or other disciplines.  In addition, course must cover perspectives (e.g. theoretical; historical; social; political; economic; cultural; religious; geographic or sense of place; environment; or intellectual traditions and/or ways of knowing) of non-Western peoples.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit.

Subject: CCJ
Title: HOLLYWOOD AND CRIME - HONORS
LongTitle: HOLLYWOOD AND THE SOCIAL CONSTRUCTION OF CRIME AND JUSTICE - HONORS
Description: Analyses movie constructions of crime and justice, and investigates the dominant images and messages prevalent in American crime cinema.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: UNDERGRAD RESEARCH - HONORS
Description: Original research under the supervision of a research adviser.  Department consent required.  Letter grade or pass-fail.  May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit.

Subject: ART
Title: FIGURE PAINTING
Description: Figurative work in oil and/or other media from studio models and/or studies from life outside the studio. 5 hrs. studio. Letter grade only. May be repeated for a maximum of 6 units.

Subject: BIO
Title: EXERCISE PHYSIOLOGY LAB
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 192, BIO 201, BIO 202, and CHM 151
Description: Lab methods in exercise physiology, including cardiopulmonary and musculoskeletal function, body composition, and flexibility. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: PHYSIOLOGY OF EXERCISE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 192, BIO 201, BIO 202, and CHM 151
Description: Physiology of human performance during exercise, including cellular and systemic responses, environmental, and training program considerations. Letter grade only.

Subject: DH
Title: ORAL HLTH, DZ PREV, NUTR
LongTitle: ORAL HEALTH, DISEASE PREVENTION, AND NUTRITION
Description: Overview of the field of dentistry, oral health, and oral disease prevention, including cariology, periodontal disease, and cancer. Letter grade only.

Subject: DH
Title: ORAL HSTLGY EMBRLGY & DNTL ANT
LongTitle: ORAL HISTOLOGY,  EMBRYOLOGY, AND DENTAL ANATOMY
Description: Foundations of orofacial development and associated structures of the head and neck anatomy, with emphasis on the embryological development of dentition and tooth-associated tissues. Letter grade only.

Subject: DH
Title: HEAD & NECK ANATOMY
Description: Study of gross structures and tissues of the human head/neck, including functional anatomy, anatomy of teeth and oral structure. Letter grade only.

Subject: DH
Title: PRECLINICAL DENTAL HYGIENE
Description: Preparation for clinical practice of dental hygiene. Theory and practice of dental hygiene on mannequins and partners, emphasizing prevention and individualized care. 2 hrs. lecture; 9 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: FRE
Title: INTERMED FRENCH COMP
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((FRE 202 or equivalent) and Foundation English Requirement); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Continuation of FRE 303. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: MATH OF FINANCE MODELING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 239 with a grade of C or better
Description: Mathematical concepts of financial models used to describe the monetary-world phenomena of random markets. Provides experience in creating and analyzing such models. Co-convened with MAT 580. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHI
Title: EPISTEMOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: 3 units of PHI-Philosophy coursework or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Issues concerning claims that one can or cannot know something. Sample issues include trust, relativism, skepticism, reason, experience, and belief. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHI
Title: TOPICS IN ETHICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: 3 units of PHI-Philosophy coursework or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Specific topic to be announced in the Schedule of Classes.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit as topic varies.

Subject: BSC
Title: IMMUNOBIOLOGY
Description: Basic aspects of immunobiology emphasizing immune response, antibody structure and reactions with antigens, immunological specificity, and immunological diseases.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ACC
Title: LANGUAGE FOR BUSINESS LAW
Description: This is language- and content-enrichment course that is linked to ACC 205: Introduction to Business Law. The goal of this course is to help students better understand the language of U. S. business by reinforcing the content and concepts of ACC 205.  Department consent required.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MKT
Title: CONCEPTS IN MARKETING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Junior Status or higher. Not open to Business majors.
Description: Overview of high-level marketing concepts and practices to provide foundational skills for those without a business background. Topics include the impact of external forces on marketing decisions; segmentation, targeting, and positioning; marketing strategies across the marketing mix; and role of marketing in social and environmental progress. (Note: This course will not count to meet major specific requirements for students pursuing a degree in accountancy, business analytics, economics, finance, information systems, management or marketing). Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: PSYCHOLOGICAL ASSESSMENT
Description: This course is designed to provide students with knowledge and skills in the selection, administration, scoring and interpretation of psychological measures of personality (adolescents and adults). Students will demonstrate skills in writing case evaluation reports and communicating results to consumers. Letter grade only.

Subject: SE
Title: CAPSTONE EXPERIENCE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: SE 476 with a grade of C or better
Description: Implementation of sponsor-accepted proposal culminating in an oral presentation, product demonstration, and formal report. Topics include project management, software architecture and design, software implementation, testing, and documentation. Must be taken in the year in which you graduate. Cross-listed with CS 486C. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: PR
Title: PR PROJ & CAMPAIGNS - HONORS
LongTitle: PUBLIC RELATIONS PROJECTS AND CAMPAIGNS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: PR 371, PR 372W, (STR 307 or STR 373), and Honors Student Group
Description: Design and practical application of research methods and public relations tactics in the context of public relations projects and campaigns. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: HA
Title: WINES OF THE WORLD - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Completed 45 units of coursework; Hotel & Restaurant Mgt BS or Minor; Honors Student Group; students must be 21+ years of age as of the first day of class
Description: This course covers the basics of many aspects of wine in a fun and educational setting. Students will learn how to taste wine, match it with food, and how to select wine. The course will cover wines from California, France, Italy, and Germany. Instructor consent required. Letter grade only.

Subject: JLS
Title: BASIC REPORTING
Description: An introduction to the elements of reporting: gathering information and interviewing people in order to create a compelling nonfiction story. Covers editing. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: MOLECULAR GENETICS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 181, BIO 182; ((CHM 230 and CHM 230L) or (CHM 235 and CHM 238) or CHM 360); and Honors Student Group
Description: Studies genetics from a molecular and microbial perspective; gene structure, expression, control, mutation, and recombination; advances in genetic engineering. Letter grade only.

Subject: MKT
Title: MARKETING RESEARCH
Description: Conceptual and practical considerations in conducting and using marketing research in decision making. Topics include problem definition, questionnaire design, data collection, sampling, data analysis, and use of scanner data. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: PATHOPHYSIOLOGY FOR NUR PRACT
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Admission to TBSN/CBSN
Corequisite:
TBSN: (NUR 322, NUR 322L, NUR 323, NUR 326, NUR 330, and NUR 355)
CBSN: (NUR 322, NUR 322L, NUR 323, NUR 326, NUR 330, NUR 352, and NUR 355)
Description: Drawing from foundational anatomy and physiology knowledge, this course will cover the study of molecular, cellular, and organ system alterations that contribute to and result from disease processes. Students will explore disease processes using a systems-based approach to understand epidemiology, risk factors, etiology, diagnosis, complications, and sequalae of disease states throughout the lifespan. Pathophysiology of disease states will be examined in relation to nursing process, clinical judgment, and professional nursing practice. Letter grade only.

Subject: STA
Title: INTERMEDIATE STATS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (STA 270 or STA 275 with a grade of C or better); and Honors Student Group
Description: Applies statistical methods to real world problems. Simple and multiple regression, ANOVA, experimental design, and categorical data analysis. Designing studies and collecting data, analyzing information, and writing reports. Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: RESORT OPERATIONS - HONORS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Honors Student Group;
Admission to Hotel & Restaurant Mgt (BS), Hospitality Leadership 90-30 (BS), Hotel & Restaurant Mgt Minor, Parks & Recreation Mgt (BS), Business Hospitality (CERT); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Material covers the history and development of resort destinations and the daily aspects of managing more diverse food, beverage, and catering operations, as well as golf, spa, timeshare, and resort operations. Letter grade only.

Subject: ME
Title: WIND ENERGY EGR - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Admission to Mechanical Engineering MS, Mechanical Engineering PhD, Bioengineering PhD or Climate Science and Solutions MS) and Honors Student Group
Description: Students will develop an understanding of the principles underlying design and operation of wind energy conversion systems (WECS), focusing on horizontal-axis turbines. The course begins with an overview of the wind industry, followed by study of the wind, its origin, magnitude and statistical description. Turbine efficiency and the Betz limit will be derived.  Rotor blade aerodynamics will be addressed in detail including Betz-optimum rotor blade shape and rotor blade performance using blade element momentum theory. Following this in-depth description of the wind turbine rotor blades will be a survey of several of the most important aspects of WECS, including: mechanics and dynamics; moments and natural frequencies; electrical power generation and grid interconnection; materials and components; wind turbine control; wind power plant design; environmental and regulatory considerations; and wind energy economics. Students will become familiar with the meaning and importance of key para

Subject: HIS
Title: TOPICS IN HISTORY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement and 6 units of HIS coursework; and Honors Student Group. Open to History BA or BS, or History and Social Studies Education majors only.
Description: Covers various regional and thematic topics in the history of Europe, Asia, Latin America, and the United States. Also includes specific instruction in historical writing. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. It may not be used to fulfill other 300-level course requirements for the History degrees. Letter grade only.

Subject: ET
Title: THIN FILM & LITHO METROLOGY
Description: Thin film deposition techniques (sputtering, evaporation, CVD) are covered and associated metrology practices are discussed. X-ray spectroscopy, including XPS and XRD to study elemental composition, chemical states, and crystal structure are explored. The fundamentals of optical/electron beam lithography and related measurements, including critical dimension are discussed. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: INSTRUMENTAL ANALYSIS
Description: Spectroscopic, electrochemical, and separation science instrumental techniques and applications.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: INDEPENDENT STUDY
Description: Individualized approach to selected topics by guided reading and critical evaluation under supervision and approval of an instructor. Department consent required. Pass/Fail only. No repeat limit. Please complete the Independent Study Proposal form and obtain signatures of approval BEFORE registering.  https://nau.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/Independent_Study_Form_FINAL-3.pdf

Subject: FOR
Title: ADV INTERNATIONAL FORESTRY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: One FOR-Forestry or BIO-Biology course
Description: Ecology, management, and policy issues related to forests around the world, including ecological, political, economic and cultural perspectives.  Co-convenes with FOR 415.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: GENERAL CHEMISTRY II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  CHM 151 with a grade of C or better
Description: Continuation of CHM 151. Letter grade only. Recommended: CHM 152L.

Subject: HA
Title: DESIGN & LAYOUT REST FACILTIES
LongTitle: DESIGN AND LAYOUT FOR RESTAURANT FACILITIES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: HA 240, HA 243, and (Hotel & Restaurant Mgt BS)
Description: This course introduces the design and layout of food service facilities to assist future managers in working with design professionals. It covers preliminary planning, the roles and responsibilities of members of the project team, the design sequence, principles of design, space analysis, equipment layout, fabricated and manufactured equipment, and engineering and architecture for food service facilities. Letter grade only.

Subject: AS
Title: AIR FORCE PHYSICAL TRNG V
LongTitle: AIR FORCE PHYSICAL TRAINING COURSE V
Description: Opportunity for NAU students who meet the following requirements: Per 10 U.S.C.2109(c)(1), Practical Military Training, "A person who is not qualified for, and...will not be able to become qualified for, advanced training (POC),...shall not be permitted to participate in...practical military training," including Physical Fitness Training. All students must have a recent physical examination medically clearing individual to participate in class and proof of liability insurance. The syllabus will cover both cardiovascular and anaerobic activity, aimed at helping every participant to get in better shape regardless of fitness level. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for a maximum of 2 units.

Subject: SOC
Title: MACROSOCIOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Any 100- or 200-level Sociology (SOC) course); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Comparative exploration of structure and change in human societies. Focuses on large-scale social patterns: subsistence, strategies, stratification, and institutions. Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: THE SOCIOLOGY OF POP CULTURE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Any 100- or 200-level Sociology (SOC) course); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: This class explores the social implications of popular culture. Students examine how popular culture is produced, consumed, interpreted and experienced. Students are introduced to classical and contemporary sociological perspectives on culture, with an emphasis on current cultural forms. We will examine various media, such as television, film, music, and the Internet and how popular culture is linked with identities, desires, and social practices.  Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: SOCIOLOGY OF COMMUNITY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Any 100- or 200-level Sociology (SOC) course); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Examines community from a number of perspectives, including social system, normative, human ecological, and social constructionist models. Images of ideal communities are contrasted with existing community forms. Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: POWER, WEALTH & INEQUALITY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Any 100- or 200-level Sociology (SOC) course); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Types and consequences of social inequality; social class, status, and power as determinants of behavior, values, and life chances; social mobility in modern society. Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: SOCIOLOGY OF ORGANIZATIONS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Any 100- or 200-level Sociology (SOC) course); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: A look at organizations function in and impact on contemporary society, as well as at the experience of the individual within the organization. Topics such as culture, work, decision-making, management, gender, race, cooperation, and deviance are examined from an organizational perspective. Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: SOCIAL PSYCHOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Any 100- or 200-level Sociology (SOC) course); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Individual and group behavior; processes of social interaction; social influences and attraction; and the development of the self and social order. Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: SOCIOLOGY OF SOCIAL MOVEMENTS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Any 100- or 200-level Sociology (SOC) course); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: This is an interdisciplinary course that will introduce students to the study of social movements. We will explore the complicated relationship between state, civil society, marginal social groups, national identity, the economy, the market and globalization. We will study both traditional and contemporary approaches to social movements, the dynamics, networks and organization of these movements, and the social impact of their activism. We will study several US movements, such as, Civil Rights, Women Rights, and Labor Movements, as well as other global movements, such as, Fair Trade, World Social Forum, Autonomia and Hacktivism. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: ADV EVOLUTION THEORY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BIO 244 and (BIO 240 or BIO 340) and Honors Student Group
Description: Advanced concepts in evolution including the origin of variation, natural selection, population genetics, mathematical approaches to understanding evolution including neutral theory, species concepts, patterns and rates of lineage diversification, and methods of phylogenetic analysis.  Letter grade only.

Subject: HS
Title: HUMAN DISEASES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: HS 200 with a grade of C or better, and Honors student group
Description: Overview of acute and chronic diseases, how these diseases affect the human body, and actions one might take to reduce the risk. Letter grade only.

Subject: HS
Title: MTRNL CHLD & SEX HLTH - HONORS
LongTitle: MATERNAL, CHILD, AND SEXUAL HEALTH - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: HS 200 with a grade of C or better, and Honors student group
Description: Introduces major issues and trends that affect the health and well-being of women, infants, children, and adolescents in domestic and international settings.  Examines healthy relationships, contraception, and STI prevention.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: UNDERGRAD RESEARCH - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  PSY 101, PSY 230 and PSY 302W and Honors Student Group
Description: Original research under the supervision and approval of a research adviser.  Course may be taken for up to 6 units per semester and may be repeated for maximum of 12 units.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: BIO
Title: FUNCTNL ANATOMY & KIN - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 125 or Math Placement Test Results (ALEKS/MATHA 65+; MATHC 65+; PLACE 70+)) and BIO 192 and BIO 201 and Honors Student Group
Description: Anatomical and mechanical basis of the science of human movement with application to analysis of movement activities.  Letter grade only.

Subject: SW
Title: ADVANCED TOPICS IN SOCIAL WORK
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to a graduate MSW program (SWMSW or SWADVMSW); ((SWMSW and SW 595) or SWADVMSW)
Description: Studies contemporary issues and topics relevant to advanced generalist social work practice. Some topics include social work in health settings, social work and aging, social work with Native Americans, and child welfare services. Master of Social Work students must earn at least six credit hours with different content. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: WRITING IN THE BIOL SCIENCES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement; (BIO 201L, BIO 205L, BIO 226L, BIO 325L, or BIO 338L); and (Junior status or higher)
Description: This course provides students with practice in biological writing for both technical and non-technical audiences, with the goal of fostering development of effective communication of scientific information by majors in the biological sciences. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: STA
Title: INTRODUCTION TO R
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Any Statistics course with a grade of C or better, or 
Corequisite:  Any graduate statistics course
Description: An introduction to the software package R. Topics include data structures (vectors, data frames), simple data manipulation, graphing data, statistical model syntax, and workflow for reproducible data manipulation and analysis. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: ME
Title: SYSTEM DYNAMICS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:
MAT 239 and ME 252 
Mech Egr BS: CS 122, and ME Fdtnl GPS Milestone
Mech & Robot Egr BS: CS 126

All prerequisites must have grades of C or better
Description: An introductory course in the theory and applications of linear system dynamics. Course topics include modeling and analysis techniques for single- and multidegree of freedom systems. Analysis techniques include Laplace transformations, transfer-functions, state-space representation, and computer simulation methods. Feedback control methods are introduced. Letter grade only.

Subject: EMGT
Title: SENIOR CAPSTONE IN EMGT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any junior-level writing course and Senior status
Description: The Emergency Management program prepares students for careers in public safety and emergency management. To ensure these goals have been met, capstone students will review the programs coursework and complete a comprehensive research project. Students will also investigate current job opportunities and requirements. Letter grade only.

Subject: AHB
Title: APPL HUM BEH PRTFOLIO CAPSTONE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any junior-level writing course and Senior status
Description: This course provides students with the opportunity to think critically about their learning experiences in the Applied Human Behavior program and reflect on how their education at Northern Arizona University has prepared them for meeting the critical challenges of direct practice and professional administrative settings. Students will develop a three-part portfolio capstone project that analyzes how the program artifacts they have created in the AHB program relate specifically to core program objectives and have prepared them for entering the professional community. Students will then assemble a creative representation of their personal growth during the program. The portfolio project encompasses the conception, planning, and development of a rationale essay, creative project, and portfolio presentation. Students are asked to participate in teamwork, develop draft assignments, engage in constructive peer feedback sessions, and prepare effective written and oral presentations. Portfoli

Subject: AIS
Title: INDIGENOUS NATION-BUILDING II
Description: Involves a senior capstone project that applies the concepts of capacity-building for indigenous nations in the current era.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: SENSATION PERCEPTION - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (PSY 255 or PSY 260); (PSY 290 or PSY 302W); and Honors Student Group
Description: Basic methods, research, and theory in vision and other senses. Topics may include form, depth, and motion perception; color vision; touch and auditory perception. Letter grade only.

Subject: ECO
Title: ENV ECO & CLIMATE CHG - HONORS
LongTitle: ENVIRONMENTAL ECONOMICS AND CLIMATE CHANGE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ECO 284; (Junior status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: This course introduces learners to the methods economists use to analyze issues related to the environment. The focus is on theories of externalities, property rights, public goods, sustainability, techniques that are used to value the environment, and approaches - such as regulation and market-based programs - that are used to control pollution. Letter grade only.

Subject: CPP
Title: PROJECTIVE PERSONALITY ASSMT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CPP 737 and admission to the Clinical Psychology Psy.D. program
Description: This course covers the Exner Comprehensive System for the Rorschach Ink Blot Test as well as selected projective tests. In addition to understanding theoretical underpinnings, the student is expected to develop some competency in the administration, scoring, & interpretation of these instruments. Letter grade only.

Subject: ITA
Title: SECOND YEAR ITALIAN - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ITA 201 and Honors Student Group
Description: Continuation of ITA 201. Letter grade only.

Subject: GSP
Title: PROGRAMMING FOR GIS
Description: This course introduces Python programming for ArcGIS Pro. It teaches Python programming and the Esri arcpy site package, and trains students to use Python scripting to automate geoprocessing and develop new GIS capabilities. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: CENE
Title: WTR & WWTR UNIT DSGN - HONORS
LongTitle: WATER AND WASTE-WATER UNITS DESIGN - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CENE 335 and CENE 386W with grades of C or better); Honors Student Group
Description: Required for environmental engineering majors. Students will design drinking water and wastewater treatment plants. This course applies principles of microbiology and water chemistry and includes treatment plant design, disinfection techniques, and water reuse. Letter grade only.

Subject: EE
Title: MODERN CONTROL SYSTEMS
Description: Modern control theory, including modeling and control analysis and design in the state space, state observer/Kalman filter, an introduction to optimal control, an introduction to optimization and model predictive control. Requires knowledge of calculus, linear algebra, differential equations, and feedback control theory in the frequency domain. Letter grade only.

Subject: ET
Title: MANUFACTURING PROCESSES II
Description: Introduction of concepts and skills for manufacturing processes. Topics include machining, boring processes, CNC processes, nano and micro-manufacturing processes, additive processes, automation, industrial robots, welding, lean engineering, and the production system. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: COUNSELING THEORIES & PROCESS
LongTitle: COUNSELING THEORIES AND PROCESSES IN SCHOOL-BASED SETTINGS
Description: This course is designed to provide students with an overview of the theoretical approaches and processes associated with psychotherapy involving K-12 students in school-based settings. Students will simultaneously learn basic counseling skills, which are emphasized in a given sequence, and learn theoretical approaches that are used by practicing school psychologists. Specifically, the course content aims at providing an overview of theoretical constructs, processes, applications, research, and assessment. Issues related to the counseling of students and families from various cultural and ethnic groups are integrated throughout. This course includes at least ten hours of supervised counseling practice. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: AGILE PROJECT MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((BBA 300 and BBA 305W); (MAT 114 or higher); and English Foundation Requirement); or admission to Project Management certificate
Description: This course introduces learners to Agile methods of project management. Beginning with an introduction to the core philosophy of Agile and the basic structure of an Agile project. The course addresses specific challenges that may be encountered by teams using Agile, best practices for integrating Agile methods into Waterfall environments, and the principles of Scrum. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: SYSTEMS CONCEPTS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((BBA 300 and BBA 305W); (MAT 114 or higher); and English Foundation Requirement); or admission to Project Management certificate
Description: This course provides students with an initial conceptual toolset to bridge disciplinary modes of thinking. It introduces systems thinking frameworks as mental devices to illuminate and critically interrogate key concepts, assumptions, frameworks and modes of engagement. Letter grade only.

Subject: GSP
Title: ENTERPRISE GEODATABASES
LongTitle: ENTERPRISE GEODATABASES AND GIS PROJECT MANAGEMENT
Description: Enterprise geodatabases have been important data repositories for large organizations. The course introduces the principles, design, and development of enterprise geodatabases for municipal systems, development of GIS applications based on geodatabases, as well as management of GIS projects. SQL Server database, ArcGIS Pro, and real-world data are used in hands-on labs. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: GSP
Title: GEOVISUALIZATION
Description: This course focuses on examination of concepts, techniques, issues and applications of analytical cartography, interactive mapping, and scientific visualization of geographic data. The course introduces concepts and theories of geovisualization. A significant portion of the course is devoted to acquiring and developing specific technical skills related to particular software packages, including ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, and others. Broadly, students will learn the art of data visualization, in terms of how to understand data as well as how to create powerful and effective communication using data. Letter grade only.
