Also involves instructional program and staff development, facilities and resource management, managing change processes, and grant writing.  Field component.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ETC
Title: DESIGNING INSTRUCTION
Description: Focuses on researching a variety of instructional design models and strategies and the application of these principles to educational learning environments with special emphasis on the design of web-based instruction.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CSD
Title: NEUR FN SPCH/LANG/HRG - HONORS
LongTitle: NEUROLOGICAL FOUNDATIONS OF SPEECH, LANGUAGE AND HEARING - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((CSD 251 or SST 251), or (Admission to Summers-Only Track or Admission to Full-Time Track, or Admission to Full-Time Leveler Track)) and Honors Student Group
Description: Applies the principles of neuroanatomy and neurophysiology to the study of normal human communication.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NTS
Title: NUTRITION RESEARCH & COMM
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((NTS 135, (PSY 230 or STA 270), and HS 200) with grades of C or better); and Foundation English Requirement
Description: In-depth study of current research methods and communication as it relates to nutrition and foods. Students will learn to identify research designs, samples and analysis plans, and types of instruments commonly used in nutrition research. Students will also learn to tailor communications for diverse audiences across a range of media. his course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: SW
Title: DIR PRCT MENT & BEH HLTH W/ADU
LongTitle: DIRECT PRACTICE IN MENTAL & BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WITH ADULT AND AGING POPULATIONS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Admission to a graduate MSW program, SW 622
Corequisite: SW 696
Description: This course will explore mental health issues as they apply to adults and the aging population. Students will learn how to engage asses, treat and evaluate diverse client systems in diverse professional settings. Letter grade only.

Subject: CENE
Title: APPLIED MECHANICS STATICS
Prerequisites:
For CENE majors and minors: (CENE 151L with a grade of Pass or MAT 136 with a grade of B or better) and (PHY 161 and MAT 137 with grades of C or better).   
All other majors: (PHY 161 and MAT 137 with grades of C or better).
Description: Fundamentals of applied mechanics, vector algebra, equivalent force systems, equations of equilibrium, structures, centroids, moments of inertia. Letter grade only.

Subject: SPA
Title: SPANISH LINGUISTICS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (SPA 304W or SPA 312W), and Honors Student Group
Description: Surveys Spanish linguistics: phonetics, phonology, morphology, and syntax, with attention also given to historical, geographical, and social variations. Letter grade only.

Subject: CCJ
Title: DOMESTIC VLNC & CJ - HONORS
LongTitle: DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND CRIMINAL JUSTICE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CCJ 101 or SOC 101) and Honors Student Group
Description: Social and historical analysis of domestic violence and the criminal justice response to that violence. Letter grade only.

Subject: APMS
Title: QUANTUM MECHANICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Applied Physics (MS) or Applied Physics & Materials Science (PhD) plan
Description: Quantum mechanics is one of the foundational pillars of modern physics. Much of modern research ranging from particle physics to condensed matter, the latter of which is the largest area of physics research, is based upon this topic. Therefore, it is an essential part of a physics students education. APMS 571 builds the technical foundation and mathematical background for the theory. We will discuss fundamental physical principles and develop the analytical tools to apply these principles to various problems. These may include but are not limited to the postulates and tools of quantum mechanics, Dirac notation, barrier problems, the harmonic oscillator, and the hydrogen atom. Co-convened with PHY 471. Letter grade only.

Subject: APMS
Title: ADVANCED QUANTUM MECHANICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Applied Physics (MS) or Applied Physics & Materials Science (PhD) plan
Description: Advanced Quantum mechanics is one of the foundational pillars of modern physics. Much of modern research ranging from particle physics to condensed matter, the latter of which is the largest area of physics research, is based upon this topic. Therefore, it is an essential part of a physics students education. APMS 571 builds the technical foundation and mathematical background for the theory, while this class, Advanced Quantum Mechanics, introduces the most important topics for understanding quantum physics. We will therefore be continuing to develop and expand upon introductory ideas as well as introduce additional advanced concepts. These may include but are not limited to approximation methods (including variational methods, and perturbation theory), angular momentum (including spin, orbital, and total angular momentum), pure states/mixed states, many-particle systems, scattering theory, and various applications to atomic, molecular, solid-state, and nuclear physics. Co-convened w

Subject: CHM
Title: GEN ORGANIC CHEMISTRY I REC
Description: Optional recitation section covering problem-solving techniques in organic chemistry.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: EE
Title: ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  MAT 136 or higher with a grade of C or better or Corequisite:  MAT 136 or higher
Description: Introduces electrical engineering including DC and AC circuit analysis, operational amplifiers, transducers, transformers, and AC power. Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: ABNORMAL PSYCHOLOGY
Description: Abnormal Psychology is the study of abnormal or dysfunctional emotions, thoughts, and behaviors.  The course focuses on experiences (symptoms) that deviate from cultural norms and cause clinically significant distress as well as psychosocial dysfunction. We will focus on the classification of psychological disorders into various classes of disorders, the disorders themselves, and symptoms of each disorder.  Other major components involve learning about the etiology (risk factors for) and research-supported treatments for psychological disorders.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: UNDERGRD TEACHING APPRNTC
Description: Teaching apprenticeship under the supervision and approval of an instructor.  Instructor consent required. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: ANT
Title: APPLYING ANTHROPOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Admission to Anthropology (BA) or Museum Studies minor); and Senior status or higher
Description: The Applying Anthropology Senior Capstone facilitates students in reflection and demonstration of their accumulated experiences in the anthropology degree program. As a culminating exercise, students will investigate how a four-field anthropological lens can be applied in practical settings with local, regional, and global communities. Students will also prepare their materials for future career opportunities and graduate work. Letter grade only.

Subject: HIS
Title: MEDCN, CULTRS, VALUES - HONORS
LongTitle: TOPICS IN MEDICINE, CULTURES, VALUES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any 300-level HIS course, and Honors Student Group
Description: Topics in the social history of medicine exploring cultural diversity and ethics in health care and policy from a comparative perspective. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit.

Subject: HIS
Title: LATIN AMERICAN HIST - HONORS
LongTitle: TOPICS IN LATIN AMERICAN HISTORY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any 300-level HIS course, and Honors Student Group
Description: Selected topics in the history of colonial and modern Latin American History from a thematic or comparative perspective.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit.

Subject: HIS
Title: RACE & ETHNCY IN U S - HONORS
LongTitle: HISTORY OF RACE AND ETHNICITY IN THE UNITED STATES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any 300-level HIS course, and Honors Student Group
Description: Examines how the concepts of race and ethnicity developed historically in the United States, and how they impacted the nations diverse populations and social structure.  Letter grade only.

Subject: HIS
Title: STUDIES IN WORLD HIST - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any 300-level HIS course, and Honors Student Group
Description: Selected topics in a world history perspective, including cross-cultural social contacts, trade, pandemics, famines, global change, and maritime endeavors.  Taught alternately as separate topics.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit.

Subject: NUR
Title: FDTNS OF NURSING PRACTICUM
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  Admission to the accelerated nursing program  
Corequisite:  NUR 331, NUR 333, NUR 333L, and NUR 336
Description: Required clinical practicum for development and practice of the fundamentals of nursing practice including psychomotor skill development needed to implement safe and effective patient care. Emphasis is placed on practice completing health and physical assessments, recognizing expected findings in patients in the laboratory and clinical settings.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: RSCH MTHDS APPLIED LINGUISTICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to TESL M.A. or Applied Linguistics Ph.D.
Description: Elements of research design and statistics in the applied linguistics context. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: TOPICS IN HLTH SERVICE PSYCH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to Combined Counseling/School Psychology (PhD)
Description: The purpose of this topics course is to provide an overview of training, practice, and professional identity development as health service psychologists specializing in counseling and school psychology.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: EPS
Title: HISTORY & SYSTEMS PSYCHOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to Combined Counseling/School Psychology (PhD)
Description: The purpose of this course is to provide students with an overview of the history of psychology. The information presented in this course will provide students with an historical perspective that will enhance students understanding of current theories, as well as trends in research and clinical techniques within the helping profession. Cross-listed with CPP 706. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: SOCIAL BASES OF BEHAVIOR
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to Combined Counseling/School Psychology (PhD)
Description: This course provides a comparative analysis of the major theories of social psychology and the empirical research testing those theories. Cross-listed with CPP 708. Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: DOCTORAL PRGM SUCCESS SEMINAR
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  Acceptance into the Curriculum and Instruction Doctoral Program
Corequisite:  ECI 701
Description: ECI 711 is a seminar designed to help students new to the Curriculum and Instruction Program develop the skills, knowledge, and dispositions necessary to successfully navigate the doctoral journey. This seminar provides an overview of the PhD process. Within this seminar, students discover both the practicalities and strategies of the doctoral experience, as well as, the challenges and unwritten rules of success. Students engage with members of the Curriculum and Instruction faculty and survey the research interests of professors within the program. Relatedly, students and faculty build of a sense of community and shared purpose. The program guidelines, requirements, processes, and procedures are presented and discussed. Constructively completing ECI 711 enables doctoral students to enter into the program more confidently and begin the journey of moving from student to scholar. Letter grade only.

Subject: MER
Title: PROMOTIONAL COMM - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement with a grades of C or better; (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: Planning, producing, and evaluating written, visual, and oral presentations for the communication and merchandising professional. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: JUS
Title: CAPSTONE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Post-baccalaureate or Graduate status) and 26 units of coursework from the MOL degree plan
Description: This is a capstone course for the Master of Organizational Leadership, Criminal Justice Administration emphasis. Students will propose, conduct, and report a major project that synthesizes and applies knowledge and skills learned in the courses that comprise a students degree program. The project should be based in both program foundation work and coursework in the emphasis area. The project must have theoretical/academic, creative, and applied components. Letter grade only.

Subject: CPP
Title: CHLD & ADLSCNT PSYCHOPATHOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CPP 601 and admission to the Clinical Psychology Psy.D. program
Description: An examination of the major psychopathologies of childhood. Various theories for the etiologies of child psychopathology are considered, and the implications for diagnosis, consultation, and treatment are also addressed. Letter grade only.

Subject: CPP
Title: BASIC ASSMT & INTRVNTN SKILLS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: CPP 602, CPP 673, CPP 680, CPP 683, CPP 737
Pre- or Corequisite: CPP 683
Description: An introduction to psychological services for clinical students. It involves developing basic intervention skills across theoretical orientations in the fundamental areas of: Conducting a clinical interview, rapport building and maintenance, diagnostic assessment and therapeutic activity. Areas of fundamental psychological assessment will also be reviewed including: Basic statistics and issues of measurement, administration practice of commonly used instruments and test interpretation. Legal, ethical, cultural and professional issues will be emphasized throughout the class as well as professional writing. Letter grade only.

Subject: CPP
Title: CLINICAL RESEARCH PROJECT
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: CPP 627 and admission to the Clinical Psychology Psy.D. program
Pre- or Corequisite: CPP 727
Description: The Clinical Research Project is intended to be a scholarly work that provides students an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and thought about a particular clinical area; to demonstrate the ability to analyze methodological issues; and to produce an original piece of scholarly work in the field of clinical psychology. Assisting the student in this process is a committee of faculty members who will review the material and make recommendations and suggestions to the student. The student should plan to spend a minimum of 9 - 12 months completing the CRP. Completion of this project is required prior to starting the internship. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for a maximum of 24 units.

Subject: MAT
Title: INTRO LINEAR ALGEBRA - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 137 with a grade of C or better; Honors Student Group
Description: Systems of equations, matrices, vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues. Students may not earn credit for both MAT 216 and MAT 316. Letter grade only.

Subject: COM
Title: WRITING SCIENCE
Description: Intensive writing practice aimed at improving writing skills in professional and lay settings. The class focuses on writing techniques, story structure, and effective revision. Written products will run the gamut from short news articles to longer features, press releases, social media outreach efforts, scripts for radio and/or video, and informal science communication products such as museum or virtual displays.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: APPLIED CARDIOVASCULAR PHYS
Description: Human physiology as it relates to cardiovascular regulation during orthostasis, exercise, aging, thermal stress, and in special populations.  Co-convened with BIO 454C.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: TEACHER LEADERSHIP
Description: The primary purpose of this course is to provide teacher candidates with experiences and knowledge for addressing changes in school environments and initiating change in school environments to improve learning for all students.  Completion of this course includes successfully completing a Signature Assignment: Newsletter.  In the newsletter, teacher candidates will describe their teaching philosophy, classroom management plan, and classroom procedures specific to their content areas.  The course in inquiry based, interdisciplinary, and contextually grounded through guided experiences in school sites.  The application and discourse of technology is embedded in all secondary education courses.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDL
Title: AMERICAN INDIAN SCHL LDRSP
Description: This serves as a survey course to assess students skills and introduce administrative theories for Indian school leaders. This course builds on experiences designed to prepare individuals to assume formal and informal leadership roles in an Indian school, including articulation of individual beliefs and values as a Native educational leader and how to be an effective leader in an Indian-serving school.  Letter grade only.

Subject: COM
Title: DATA METAPHORS & VISUALIZATION
Description: Graphical displays of quantitative information take on many forms to help us understand and communicate both data and models. This course will serve to introduce students to the most common forms of scientific graphical displays (data metaphors), uses, and misuses. The class will also introduce principles of visual perception, color and design theory, and best practices for data metaphor development and visualization.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDL
Title: INTERNATIONAL EDUCATION FINANC
Description: A focus of this course is on finance theory and practice, historical developments in education funding, present sources of revenue and methods of allocating funds, and current problems in funding education systems and financing of global education as a social issue.  Students will learn strategies for effective budgeting and financing organizations necessary for effective international education experiences, both for students domestically and abroad. Students will learn about different aspects of the fundamental principles and techniques of International Education Finance and gain an understanding of effective leadership practices in this area within the field of international education.  Special emphasis is given to problems of funding education systems and international organizations. Letter grade only.

Subject: EDR
Title: INTRO TO DATA-DRIVEN RESEARCH
LongTitle: INTRODUCTION TO DATA-DRIVEN RESEARCH - AMERICAN INDIAN SCHOOL LEADERSHIP
Description: This course serves as an introduction to the concepts of data-driven methodologies. It focuses on the nature and application of quantitative and qualitative methods for Tribal school leadership decision making utilizing data driven methods. Letter grade only.

Subject: EDL
Title: CURRENT ISSUES IN INTERNATIONA
Description:
The International Education field is constantly changing based on national and international events.  These events can both originate from outside of the IE field or from within the field, and can have profound impacts on different facets of the field. How international education professionals react to these events will affect international mobility flows, internationalization strategy, and the development of a countrys higher education sector.

This course will allow students to critically analyze Current Issues in international education including their impact on the field as well as how IE professionals should react to these events.  Students will start by developing a foundational understanding of the international education field in light of such events. The course will then examine past events and their impact on the field. The course will end by asking students to research current events and recommend appropriate reactions to them. Letter grade only.

Subject: EDL
Title: INTERCULTURAL COMMUNICATION AN
Description: This course explores intercultural communication theory and practices which can be used in educational contexts in the US and internationally.  The course also examines effective leadership, management and communication practices within contexts of international education. Letter grade only.

Subject: ETC
Title: TECH INTEGRATION PK-16
LongTitle: TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION IN THE PK-16 CLASSROOM
Description: This course brings together theory, content, and technology to assist educators in developing understanding, appreciation, and a vision of technologys roles and impacts on curriculum.  This course is based on models, theories, national standards, and relevant research.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CCJ
Title: RESEARCH METHODS FOR CRIMINOLO
Description: Explores the role of the quantitative paradigm in criminal justice; examines modes of inquiry and proposal development in criminal justice.  Letter grade only.  Prerequisite: undergraduate research methods course

Subject: CCHE
Title: LEADERSHIP SKILLS
Description: Survey course to assess skills and plan the graduate program for leadership students.  Introduces administrative theory.  This course contains an assessment that must be successfully completed to be eligible to register for the internship. Letter grade only.

Subject: EDL
Title: MASTER'S SEMINAR
Description: Culminating course for M.Ed. Focuses on skill attainment and professional growth. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: PRO ENVIRONMENTAL WRITING
Description: This course will examine environmental communication, specifically green/cultural tourism promotion, journalism, public participation, health and safety, non-profit communication, and multimedia production. Students will incorporate field experience and produce a workable business/problem solving proposal, multimedia marketing or informational materials for an instructor approved non-profit organization.  Letter grade only.

Subject: COM
Title: ETHICS & STRATEGIES SCI COMM
LongTitle: ETHICS AND STRATEGIES IN SCIENCE COMMUNICATION
Description: Examination of statistical significance, the use of math skills to "translate" numbers into forms that contribute to understanding, and how quantitative reasoning tools can facilitate ethical and accurate science communication. Students learn the challenges of communicating about controversial topics in science and the role cultural considerations play in determining how stories are researched and told.  Letter grade only.

Subject: HIS
Title: TOPICS IN GRADUATE RESEARCH
Description: Research and writing using archival, primary, and secondary sources in topics appropriate to primary or secondary field.  Required of all history graduate students.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit.

Subject: ENG
Title: APPROACHES TO TEACHING GRAMMAR
Description: The purpose of the course is to prepare future secondary English language arts teachers to comfortably and confidently integrate the teaching of grammar into the language arts curriculum.  We will cover topics of grammar and usage with a focus on how to teach students these topics in engaging and authentic ways.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PT
Title: INTERV IN MUSCULSKELETL THERAP
Description: In-depth analysis of selected interventions currently used to treat clients with dysfunctions of the musculoskeletal system.  Pass-fail or letter grade.  May be repeated for up to 43 units of credit.

Subject: TSM
Title: SUPERVISED STDNT TCH EXP
LongTitle: SUPERVISED STUDENT TEACHING EXPERIENCE: SECONDARY MATHEMATICS OR SCIENCE SEMINAR
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  Admission to Apprenticeship or Student Teaching
Corequisite:  TSM 495C
Description: Required of mathematics and science secondary education majors. Students reflect and are supported in their supervised (student) teaching experiences and signature assessments. Co-convened with TSM 596. Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: PROG FOR EGR & SCI LAB
LongTitle: PROGRAMMING FOR ENGINEERING AND SCIENCE LAB
Description: Provides guided practical experience with applied engineering and science-oriented programming problems. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: BIOCHEMISTRY I - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BIO 181 and CHM 238 and Honors Student Group
Description: Chemistry of biological substances and the production and use of energy in biochemical systems.  Credit will not be given for both CHM 360 and CHM 461.  Letter grade only.

Subject: JUS
Title: SR CAPSTONE IN JUSTICE STUDIES
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: JUS 350W and Senior Status  
Corequisite:  JUS 330, (JUS 411 or INT 440), (JUS 450 or JUS 452)
Description: The Justice Studies program prepares students to ethically work and lead in the criminal justice or intelligence studies fields. To ensure these goals have been met, students in this capstone course will (1) review the programs coursework, (2) complete a comprehensive research project in their field of study, and (3) investigate the current job requirements and opportunities.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: PLANTS AND CLIMATE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 181, Senior status, and Honors Student Group
Description: Analyzes plant physiological functions, including photosynthesis, transpiration, respiration, and energy balance, and interactions with the past and current climate system.  3 hours lecture.  Recommended:  CHM 360, BIO 284.  Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: HOTEL OPERATIONS & MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: ((Hotel & Restaurant Mgt (BS) or Hotel & Restaurant Mgt Minor or Business Hospitality (CERT) or Parks & Recreation Mgt (BS) or Arts & Cultural Minor)) or International Exchange Student Group
Pre- or Co requisite: HA 100
Description: This course studies the role of the General Manager and all key departments of the lodging operation. Letter grade only.

Subject: LAN
Title: GLOBAL ENGAGEMENT ABROAD
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Interdisciplinary Global Program (IGP)
Description: This asynchronous online course facilitates an exploration of students learning-immersion experience while abroad, particularly with respect to cultural and social expectations in academic, professional, and personal spheres of the host culture. The course provides a guided structure for researching, reflecting on, journaling/blogging, and communicating about immersive academic experiences abroad by promoting dialogue across disciplines and cultures via online communication. Additionally, students will engage in direct research through engagement in the society in which they are living and studying in order to help prepare them for their field work experiences during the second semester abroad. Participation is limited to, and required of, students in the Interdisciplinary Global Programs (IGP) who are currently studying and working abroad (normally the fourth year of the program). Letter grade only.

Subject: ID
Title: INTERIOR DESIGN I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (ID 221 or ID 261 with a grade of C or better) or Interior Design Milestone
Description: Studio course. Basic problems in design of total interior spaces. Emphasizes determinants of space utilization and specifications and preparation of design graphics. Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: PSY OF DECISION MKING - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (PSY 260 and (PSY 290 or PSY 302W)); Honors Student Group
Description: Covers experimental studies of human judgement and decision-making processes, with a special interest in understanding how research data challenge the assumption that people make decisions as rational agents. Topics include mental shortcuts, misweighting of factors, framing effects, and misconceptions regarding chance/probability. Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: LANGUAGE & COGNITION - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (PSY 260 and (PSY 290 or PSY 302W)); Honors Student Group
Description: Theory and research on the role of language in human thought and behavior with attention to brain mechanisms, speech, comprehension, reading, writing, second language acquisition, and social context. Letter grade only.

Subject: SW
Title: FOUNDATION SW PRACTICE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Social Work - Advanced Standing MSW program
Description: The course will provide conceptual, theoretical, and empirical knowledge related to direct practice strategies across client systems including individual, families, groups, communities and organizations. Additionally, this course will examine and discuss key direct practice concepts and social work frameworks that inform culturally competent social work practice across various client systems. Letter grade only.

Subject: PR
Title: PR CASE STDS & RSRCH - HONORS
LongTitle: PUBLIC RELATIONS CASE STUDIES AND RESEARCH - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: STR 272 and Honors Student Group
Description: Case-study method of analysis of contemporary public relations problems for industry, nonprofit organizations, and governmental agencies. Research emphasized. Letter grade only.

Subject: ACC
Title: ACCOUNTING ETHICS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Business Professional Program Milestone and Honors Student Group
Description: This course is a study of accounting ethics, related professional responsibilities and their relationships with business issues. It covers the identification of ethical issues, analysis of the issues using a multiple frameworks and theories, and development of conclusions and action items to resolve those issues. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: ADULT HEALTH & ILLNESS II
Description: This course continues the focus on nursing care of adults experiencing health-illness transitions. Letter grade only.

Subject: NTS
Title: NUTRITION & METABOLSM - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: NTS 250, BIO 201, BIO 202, (CHM 130 or CHM 151); and Honors Student Group
Description: This course provides an in-depth study of nutrition and foods as they relate to health and pathophysiology of disease, which includes anatomy/physiology, genetics, and nutrient metabolism. Letter grade only

Subject: ESE
Title: PROFESSIONAL DEV'T SEMINAR
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ESE 571, ESE 572, ESE 573, and ESE 574
Description: Designed to assist in preparing you for entry into your professional arena. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: FOR
Title: FIRE MONITORING & MEASUREMENTS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement; FOR 251, STA 270
Description: This course serves as a crucial component to comprehensive learning in fire-adapted ecosystems. It is designed to equip students with fundamental knowledge and practical skills essential for measuring and monitoring fire-adapted ecosystems and resources. The course delivers insights into basic forest measurement skills and practical applications of statistical concepts, crucial components of a successful monitoring program, and an extensive understanding of fire monitoring conventions and protocols commonly utilized by land managers. The articulated structure and content of this course enable students to gain familiarity and competence in facilitating a broader understanding and appreciation of the diverse, fire-adapted ecosystems, thereby contributing substantially to their academic and professional development in applied wildland fire science. By the completion of this course, students will not only attain theoretical acumen but also pragmatic proficiency in measuring and monitoring

Subject: PM
Title: PROJECT SCHEDULE AND CONTROL
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Organizational Leadership (M) or Mechanical Engineering (MS)
Description: Basic principles of project scheduling and control with resource allocation and management. Project control methods principally through earned value management principles and practices. CPM, PERT, Gantt scheduling with the identification of critical path and resulting floats. Resource leveling, compression, and network adjustments. Instructor consent required. Letter grade only.

Subject: PM
Title: PRJCT STRATS & METHODOLOGIES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Organizational Leadership (M) or Mechanical Engineering (MS)
Description: This course will identify and instruct students in the knowledge, concepts and skills required for a variety of critical and current project deliveries, strategies, and methodologies. Letter grade only.

Subject: PM
Title: PROJECT COST ESTIMATING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Organizational Leadership (M) or Mechanical Engineering (MS)
Description: An introduction to the science and art of project cost estimating. Particular emphasis will be placed on internal cost development and external resource costing and fundamental estimating techniques. Estimating project costs is one of the keys to successful project management. Instructors consent required. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: TRANSITION TO PRACTICE & ACJ
LongTitle: TRANSITION TO PRACTICE AND APPLIED CLINICAL JUDGEMENT
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: TBSN/CBSN: NUR 362, NUR 438L 
GBSN: NUR 362, NUR 383 
Corequisite: GBSN: NUR 443, NUR 448L, NUR 450C, NUR 460
Description: This Transition to Practice & Applied Clinical Judgment course provides the learner with an opportunity to synthesize concepts presented throughout the curriculum and to begin the transition from student to professional nurse. Emphasis is placed on applying critical thinking, clinical judgment, and evidenced-based nursing practice. Advanced simulation experiences provide opportunities to demonstrate high quality nursing care, healthcare team coordination, and the oversight and accountability for care delivery to promote safety and quality in client care. Additionally, students will generate strategies for successful practice as a novice professional nurse. They will reflect on personal beliefs and responses to entering professional practice and the importance of personal accountability and selfcare. This course explores preparing for NCLEX success and licensure. Letter grade only.

Subject: AS
Title: AIR POWER HISTORY II
Description: Establishment of Air Force as a separate service, the Cold War, development of various concepts of employment and technological improvements of air power from Korean conflict to present; effective communication skills.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: MATH OF FINANCIAL MODELING
Description: Mathematical concepts of financial models to describe the monetary-world phenomena of random markets. Provides experience in creating and analyzing such models. Co-convened with MAT 480. Letter grade only

Subject: MUP
Title: JAZZ IMPROVISATION II
Description: Continuation of MUP 241. Letter grade only.

Subject: REL
Title: THE HISTORICAL JESUS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (3 hours of REL-Religious Studies coursework and Sophomore Status or higher) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Training in the methods of modern historical-critical study of gospel materials.  Explores how the sayings and deeds of Jesus were remembered, spread, and written down.  Recommended: REL 220.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: GENERAL CHEMISTRY II - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  CHM 151 with a grade of C or better and Honors Student Group
Description: Continuation of CHM 151. Letter grade only. Recommended: CHM 152L.

Subject: EE
Title: INTRO TO DIGITAL LOGIC
Prerequisites: Pre- or Corequisite:  MAT 125 or higher with a grade of C or better or ALEKS/MATHC 65+
Description: Design of digital subsystems using individual components, MSI and LSI circuits, design of state machines. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab.  Letter grade only.

Subject: JLS
Title: ENV REPORTING TOPICS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: 
Journalism majors: JLS 231; Foundation English Requirement; and (Junior status or higher)
Other majors: Foundation English Requirement; and (Junior status or higher)
Description: Students learn the techniques, research, and function of investigative and feature storytelling as they work on environmental reporting projects. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit with different topics.

Subject: ME
Title: MACHINE DESIGN II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CS 122, CENE 253, ME 252, ME 240 and ME 365 and ME Fdtnl GPA Milestone for ME majors) or (CS 122, CENE 253, ME 252, ME 240 and ME 365 for non-ME majors)
Description: Fundamentals of mechanical design; stress, deflection, strength, lubrication, and design principles; design of mechanical elements:  helical gears, bevel gears, worm gearing, shafts, clutches, breaks, flexible mechanical elements, bearings, permanent joints. Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: FNDTNS IN KITCHEN OPS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: HA 240 and (Hotel & Restaurant Mgt BS or (Health Science Nutrition & Food BS or Minor))
Description: Application of the principles, procedures, and techniques involved in managing small- and large-quantity food and beverage preparation,  production and service. Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: HOSPITALITY LITIGATION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (HA 335 and Hotel & Restaurant Mgt (BS)) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: In-depth analysis of the litigation process both procedurally and substantively as it relates to the hospitality industry. Letter grade only.

Subject: GLG
Title: PHYSICAL GEOLOGY LAB
Description: Must be taken concurrently with GLG 101. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENV
Title: ENV CHALL & SOLUTIONS LAB
Description: Investigates the impacts of humans on the natural world while developing an appreciation of the value of scientific inquiry and the tools for scientific research. 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: HON
Title: INTRODUCTION TO HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Honors Student Group and less than 12 units earned at NAU
Description: Through an intentional active learning process, first time Honor students will critically explore an Honors education and their understanding of themselves and the world around them.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: CTE
Title: CAPSTONE IN CTE
LongTitle: CAPSTONE IN CAREER AND TECHNICAL EDUCATION
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: CTE 300, Senior Status
Pre- or Corequisite: CTE 340W, CTE 396, CTE 465, CTE 433; (CTE 431, CTE 455, or CTE 486); and (9 units from CTE 330, CTE 399, CTE 401, CTE 431, CTE 455, CTE 486, CTE 491, CTE 492, CTE 496, CTE 499, CTE 593)
Description: The integration and application of occupational experience and CIT program content. This course integrates your occupational experience with your classroom education. This course meets the capstone requirement for the CTE program. Letter grade only.

Subject: OTD
Title: PRACTICE COMPETENCY II
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  OTD 601, OTD 645, OTD 646, OTD 670, OTD 761, OTD 771  
Corequisite:  OTD 701, OTD 748, OTD 772
Description: Competency activities will be completed to synthesize learning and demonstrate reflective clinical reasoning across the semester coursework. Skills requisite for practice competency and interprofessional education activities will be included.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: BBA
Title: ADVANCED LOGISTICS
LongTitle: ADVANCED LOGISTICS AND SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (BBA 292, BBA 300, BBA 305W, and BBA 340) or LSCM Certificate students
Description: Covers logistics network design, supply chain management, procurement, supplier partnerships, inventory, transportation, warehousing, packaging, global logistics, and information management for logistics. Letter grade only.

Subject: SW
Title: FOUNDATION PRACTICE III
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  Admission to MSW program, SW 521  
Corequisite: SW 556
Description: This course provides students with essential and beginning knowledge and skills for effective social work practice with communities and organizations. Letter grade only.

Subject: ARH
Title: ITAL RENAISSANCE ART - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher), Honors Student Group
Description: Historical survey of the painting, sculpture, and architecture from the fourteenth century through the seventeenth century in Italy. Letter grade only.

Subject: CST
Title: RACE, GENDER & MEDIA - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher), Honors Student Group
Description: Examines images of gender, race, ethnicity, and other social positions in mass media, with concomitant societal implications. Letter grade only.

Subject: MGT
Title: CONCEPTS MANAGEMENT - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher), and not in Business Professional Program, and not in Business Preparation Plan, and Honors Student Group
Description: Surveys management of organizations; includes international, societal, and organizational structure issues. (Note:  If youre enrolled in a BSACCY, BSBA, or MBA program in The W.A. Franke College of Business, you cannot take this course for credit.)  Letter grade only.

Subject: CPP
Title: CONSULTATION & SUPERVISION
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Admission to the Clinical Psychology Psy.D. program
Pre- or Corequisite: CPP 702
Description: This course will provide students with the theoretical and empirical knowledge, as well as practice skills, needed to function in the professional roles of consultation and supervision. Contextual issues related to contemporary models and systems of service delivery will be considered. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: GENETICS AND EVOLUTION
Description: Fundamental concepts of inheritance, including genetic and chromosomal character determination, and natural selection leading to population changes and speciation.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: PHYCOLOGY
Description: Systematics, structure, reproduction, ecology, and methods of collecting algae; culture and experimental study of selected algae species. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: MYCOLOGY
Description: Systematics, physiology, ecology, and medical importance of fungi and related forms; field identification and experimental studies. Co-convened with BIO 511. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: ORNITHOLOGY
Description: Classification, anatomy, physiology, ecology, behavior, distribution, and field identification of birds. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: PHYCOLOGY
Description: Systematics, structure, reproduction, ecology, and methods of collecting algae; culture and experimental study of selected algae species. Field trips, 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only. Course fee required.

Subject: BIO
Title: LOWER INVERTEBRATES
Description: Surveys the "lower" invertebrate phyla using selected taxa to illustrate concepts in evolution, ecology, and behavior. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: ENTOMOLOGY
Description: Classification, identification, ecology, physiology, and economic importance of insects.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: CLASSICAL MOLECULAR GENETICS
Description: Studies genetics from a molecular and microbial perspective; gene structure, expression, control, mutation, and recombination; advances in genetic engineering.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: NATIVE PLANTS OF ARIZONA
Description: BSC 414 will provide an in depth study of the flora and vegetation types of Arizona, with an emphasis on species and vegetation found in Southwestern Arizona, and will present information on morphology, taxonomy, and natural history of important flowering plant families of Arizona. It will also involve the examination of live plant material of species discussed in lecture. We will also identify plants using keys and descriptions in standard floras. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: FUNDMNTL EVOLUTIONARY BIOLOGY
Description: Fundamentals of evolutionary theory including natural selection, adaptation, species concepts and speciation, basics of phylogenetics and population genetics as well as patterns of diversification and extinction.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: NATIVE PLANTS OF ARIZONA
Description: Emphasis on the recognition of much of the native and naturalized flora of Arizona by community from Alpine Tundra to the Sonoran Desert. Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: ECONOMIC BOTANY
Description: Surveys plants used by humans including plant parts or products used and their geographic origin and distribution. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: MARINE BIOLOGY
Description: Behavior, ecology, biogeography, and evolution of marine organisms relative to the physics and geology of the ocean. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: PLANT MORPHOLOGY
Description: Comparative functional morphology, ecology, and evolutionary relationships of algae, fungi, bryophytes, and vascular plants; field and lab exercises include fossil and living plants. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: DEVELOPMENTAL BIOLOGY
Description: Principles of development in amphibians, birds, and mammals.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: BASIC VIROLOGY
Description: Molecular and biochemical characteristics of viruses, mechanisms of infectivity, and viral diseases. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: BIOMECHANICS
Description: This course integrates anatomy, physiology and mechanics to explore the principles of human and animal movement. It highlights the diversity of techniques required to study the physics of movement. Co-convened with BIO 442.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NAV
Title: SECOND YEAR NAVAJO
Description: Continuation of NAV 201. Letter grade only.

Subject: NAV
Title: SECOND YEAR NAVAJO
Description: Review of grammar and phonetics. Development of speaking, reading, and writing skills. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: UNIV PHYSICS I RECITATION
Description: Optional recitation section covering problem-solving techniques in classical mechanics.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: ID
Title: AUTOCAD I - TWO DIMENSIONAL
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ID 121 with a grade of C or better
Description: Studio course. Basic two-dimensional drawing and editing commands, drawing setup, and plotting features. Hands-on project class, teaches the use of software through the creation of typical interior design and architectural drawings. Letter grade only.

Subject: FW
Title: PROF PRACTICE/RESPONSIBILITIES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: HS 200, BIO 192, BIO 201, BIO 202, FW 325, FW 330, FW 343, FW 420C, and any Nutrition (NTS) course
Description: This course will prepare students in the skills and knowledge needed to demonstrate professional responsibilities and practice required to work in the fitness/wellness industry. Students will learn to safely and effectively facilitate fitness programs in a variety of recreational, nonprofit, corporate and health club settings. Letter grade only.

Subject: FW
Title: STRENGTH AND CONDITIONING PROG
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 192, BIO 201, BIO 201L, and HS 200
Description: This course is designed to provide foundational knowledge of exercise training physiology and applied experience in strength training and conditioning modalities. General course topics include physiological responses and adaptations to training, program design, periodization of training, performance evaluation, speed/agility development, power training, body weight and implement training, balance and instability training, corrective exercise, plyometric training, flexibility training, all of which are important for any future exercise professional. Students will be required to demonstrate proper exercise technique and display the ability to effectively instruct others in-group settings. Students will also develop and demonstrate the skills to write safe and effective programs to enhance athletic performance and general health and fitness. Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: ED & CAREER PLAN IN PSYCH SCI
LongTitle: EDUCATIONAL AND CAREER PLANNING IN THE PSYCHOLOGICAL SCIENCES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: PSY 101 and (Psychological Sciences BS or Psychology BS)
Description: Required course designed to provide majors with the information and tools needed to help them make informed decisions about their current educational plans and future career directions. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: BME
Title: SEI METHDS SEC SCHOOL - HONORS
LongTitle: STRUCTURE ENGLISH IMMERSION METHODS FOR SECONDARY SCHOOL - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Secondary Education Milestone and Honors Student Group
Description:
This course is designed to prepare pre-service teachers to:  assess the needs of secondary English Language Learners (ELL) and develop materials and effective teaching methods.   This course contains an assessment that must be successfully completed in order to register for student teaching.  Co-convenes with BME 537. 
 Letter grade only.

Subject: CTE
Title: PROG & CURR PLANNING IN CTE
LongTitle: PROGRAM AND CURRICULUM PLANNING IN CAREER AND TECHNICAL EDUCATION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  CTE 300 and (Junior status or higher or CTE Certificate)
Description: Methods of gathering and analyzing information necessary for establishing responsive and comprehensive career and technical education programs, courses, and lessons. Co-convenes with CTE 592. Letter grade only.

Subject: NTS
Title: FUTURES IN NUTRITION & FOODS
LongTitle: FUTURES IN NUTRITION AND FOODS & DIETETICS
Description: This course provides students with the skills needed to apply for careers in the field of nutrition and dietetics, as well as graduate programs and dietetic internships. Students will learn how to market and communicate their skills and abilities pertinent to the field via resumes, cover letters, essays, applications, and interviews. Topics essential for success in the work force such as ethics and professionalism are also addressed. Letter grade only.

Subject: COM
Title: DIGITAL MEDIA MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Sophomore status or higher and School of Communication major or minor
Description: Students in this class learn best practices in digital media management, while managing digital content for a working newsroom. These practices include, writing content for the web, posting content on a website, managing a YouTube channel and promoting news content on social media. Students will also examine trends in the digital media job market and identify skills needed to succeed in that marketplace. Letter grade only.

Subject: SPA
Title: INTRO TO LIT SPANISH - HONORS
LongTitle: INTRODUCTION TO LITERATURE IN SPANISH - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (SPA 304W or SPA 312W) and Honors Student Group
Description: Modern Hispanic works (short stories, novels, poetry, and drama) are analyzed to introduce the techniques of critical evaluation of literature.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ART
Title: STUDIO EXHIBITION - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ARE 330W and Honors Student Group
Description: Studio course. Independent studio activity culminating in an exhibition, a written statement, and an oral presentation directed and evaluated by a faculty committee. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit with different content.

Subject: CST
Title: COMMUNICATION CRITICISM
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement; (COM 101, COM 200, CST 111, and CST 201); and (Junior Status or higher)
Description: Examines methods of analyzing and evaluating public discourse, including rhetorical, media, and/or cultural criticism. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: NTS
Title: NUTR FOR SPORT PERF & WELLNESS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 201, BIO 201L, BIO 202, BIO 202L, and NTS 135 with grades of C or better in each
Description: This course is a required course in the Nutrition and Foods major and also the Nutrition and Foods minor.  This course provides the link between basic nutrition and food planning content and the connection with physical activity and physical performance.  It will explore the unique energy needs of and nutritional considerations for those participating and many different athletic events.  The course will address the special nutritional and training needs of athletes and individuals who are physically active. Major topics include the function and sources of each of the nutrients, fueling for pre exercise, during exercise and after exercise, specific meal planning, nutrient needs for specific athletes and wellness lifestyles, and more. Co-convened with NTS 525. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHA
Title: PHRMCLGY PHRMCTHRPTCS I
Description: This is the first course in a two-course series. It will focus on the general principles of pharmacology and the application of these principles to pharmacotherapeutics and patient care situations. Students will learn the classes of pharmaceuticals with a focus on the mechanisms of action, common side effects, adverse reactions and interactions. At the conclusion of the two-course series, students will have been introduced to commonly prescribed medications organized using a systems-based model. Letter grade only.

Subject: EE
Title: INTERMEDIATE PROGRAMMING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  MAT 136 and (CS 122 or CS 122H or CS 126) with grades of C or better
Description: Intermediate programming using C including program design, algorithm design and data structures. Letter grade only.

Subject: CST
Title: BUS & PRO SPEAKING - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: Communication principles, procedures, and practices in organizations. Emphasizes effective oral presentation techniques in a variety of contexts including informative, persuasive, and group presentations. For non-majors only. Letter grade only.

Subject: ARH
Title: TPCS MED ART & CLTRS - HONORS
LongTitle: TOPICS IN MEDITERRANEAN ARTS AND CULTURES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: This course explores diverse topics related to the material culture of the Ancient/Classical Mediterranean world. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 9 units with different topics.

Subject: ARH
Title: CNTMPRY NATIVE ART - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: This course covers twentieth-century and contemporary North American art. Readings are paired with examination of artworks and discussion of their contexts of production and reception. Letter grade only.

Subject: ARH
Title: BAROQUE & ROCOCO ART - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: Historical survey of the painting, sculpture, and architecture from the seventeenth century through the third quarter of the eighteenth century. Letter grade only.

Subject: HUM
Title: DIGITAL HUMANITIES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: Examines the contemporary public humanities, defined as the production of humanistic meanings in public contexts, from museum work, to oral historians, government and non-profit work, as well as engagement in a wide array of community-based projects. We will consider the ramifications of the digital humanities for the communication and preservation of culture and the impact of technology on the interpersonal interactions. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 15 units of credit.

Subject: BIO
Title: ORNITHOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: Classification, anatomy, physiology, ecology, behavior, distribution, and field identification of birds. 2 hrs. lecture, 4 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: PARASITOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: Animal parasites, their life cycles, evolution, host pathologies, treatments, means of control, and prevention of infection. Letter grade only. Recommended: BIO 475L

Subject: ENV
Title: CLIMATE CHG & SOCIETY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher); and Honors Student Group
Description: This course explores the social, political, and cultural factors that collectively shape how society understands and responds to climate change. We will investigate how social and cultural aspects influence our understanding of climate change, identify the ideological positions of supporters and deniers, analyze current policies and politics, investigate differential climate vulnerability, and explore and critically analyze actions proposed to meaningfully address challenges climate change poses to society. This course will also include examining current events, new climate policies, and the climate movement. Letter grade only.

Subject: MS
Title: LEADERSHIP LAB
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Completion of documentation by first class to determine eligibility for enrollment  Corequisite:  MS 102 and MS 151
Description: Military leadership and small unit tactics.  Develops leadership abilities through practical application and challenging outdoor activities.  Letter grade only.

Subject: DH
Title: CLINIC I SEMINAR AND LABS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  DH 311 with grade of C or better
Description: Applies theory and techniques of oral hygiene therapy in a clinical environment.  Focuses on dental hygiene science with experiences in clinical procedures. 3 hrs. lecture; 9 hrs. clinic. Letter grade only.

Subject: POS
Title: TOPICS POLITICAL SCI - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (POS 100 or POS 201); 3 units of POS-Political Science coursework; 45 cumulative units; Foundation English Requirement; and Honors Student Group
Description: Analyzes contemporary issues in national and international politics, incorporating extensive writing skill instruction. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit with different content.

Subject: STA
Title: STATISTICAL COMPUTING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((STA 471 or STA 571 with a grade of C or better) and STA 445), or admission to Statistics MS
Description: Computationally intensive statistical methods. Topics include statistical learning (cross-validation, GAMs, classification and regression trees), nonparametric methods (bootstrapping and permutation tests) as well as general-purpose algorithms (MCMC). Emphasis placed on both underlying statistical concepts and implementing resulting algorithms in a high-level mathematical programing language on non-tidy data. Co-convened with STA 478. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: MANAGEMENT INFO SYSTEMS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BBA 293, ISM 120, or (Admission to Tech Management program, Accounting and Finance UCERT, or Applied Business Management BS)
Description: This course presents information systems used by organizations and work groups with an emphasis on system development, management and effective use. Letter grade only.

Subject: MKT
Title: CONCEPTS IN MARKETING - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Junior Status or higher) and Honors Student Group. Not open to Business majors.
Description: Overview of high-level marketing concepts and practices to provide foundational skills for those without a business background. Topics include the impact of external forces on marketing decisions; segmentation, targeting, and positioning; marketing strategies across the marketing mix; and role of marketing in social and environmental progress. (Note: This course will not count to meet major specific requirements for students pursuing a degree in accountancy, business analytics, economics, finance, information systems, management or marketing). Letter grade only.

Subject: SW
Title: SENIOR SEMINAR - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  SW 422, SW 423, SW 427, Social Work Milestone, Senior Status, and Honors Student Group
Description: Capstone course for integrating social work curriculum into a framework for influencing change as professional generalist social workers.  Letter grade only.

Subject: VC
Title: GRAPHIC DESIGN IV
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Graphic Design Milestone; VC 303 and VC 343 with grades of C or better
Description: Studio course. Intermediate visual problems in user experience design, information design, information architecture, data visualization, prototyping, and environmental displays of information. Intermediate software skills required. Letter grade only.

Subject: SPA
Title: SPA EVERYDAY CONVO - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: SPA 202 and Honors Student Group
Description: Emphasizes oral proficiency in Spanish through speaking, writing, reading, and listening activities. If your competence exceeds the scope of this course, you receive credit by exam. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHO
Title: EXPERIMENTAL PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Alternative photographic techniques using traditional silver halide processing and non-silver alternative processes, digital processes including cyanotypes, van dyke prints, and gum bichromate prints. Letter grade only.

Subject: NTS
Title: NUTR FOR COMMUNITY WELLNESS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: NTS 325W, NTS 335, NTS 355, NTS 415, and Senior status
Description: This course is the capstone requirement for the Nutrition & Foods degree program. Students will reflect on and synthesize important concepts and skills acquired throughout the Nutrition & Foods degree program by planning, implementing and evaluating a Community Nutrition project. The primary emphasis of this course is to apply the previous knowledge gained from the entire Nutrition & Foods degree program into an actual application project. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: SEM CREAT WRT: POETRY - HONORS
LongTitle: SEMINAR IN CREATIVE WRITING: POETRY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement,  ENG 371, and Honors Student Group
Description: Intensive study of poetry writing emphasizing the writing of a single long poem or a sequence of interrelated, shorter poems. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit with different content.

Subject: ENG
Title: WRITING FOR PERFORMANCE
Description: A creative writing workshop focused on the composition and revision of short-form student plays or other performance texts. Letter grade only.

Subject: CPP
Title: CLINICAL PSYCH PRACTICUM I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CPP 601, CPP 602, CPP 673, CPP 680, CPP 683, CPP 700, CPP 711, and CPP 737
Description: Supervised practice experience allows students to acquire and develop clinical skills in assessment and intervention. Practicum training sites provide direct clinical experience and direct, case-oriented supervision. The Practicum Seminar provides a focus on general clinical issues and skills, including diagnostic interviewing, assessment, case formulation, and psychotherapy approaches. Cultural and individual differences that effect assessment, formulation, and intervention will be discussed. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for a maximum of 6 units.

Subject: CHM
Title: FUNDAMENTAL BIOCHEMISTRY LAB
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CHM 230L or CHM 235L) and Prerequisite or Corequisite: CHM 360
Description: Introduces modern qualitative and quantitative biochemical techniques.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CMF
Title: STUDIO PRODUCTION
Description: Introduces the techniques and develops awareness and understanding of television studio production with emphasis on the function and operation of the primary tools including video, audio, and lighting. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: CONTEMP LITERATURE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement; 3 additional units of ENG-English coursework; and Honors Student Group
Description: Selected post-World War II literature. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: ANCIENT LITERATURE - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement; 3 additional units of ENG-English coursework; and Honors Student Group
Description: Representative forms of ancient literature with special attention to epic and tragedy. Letter grade only.

Subject: VC
Title: TYPOGRAPHY II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ART 135, ART 150, VC 201, and VC 202 with grades of C or better
Description: Studio course. Advanced exploration of typographic principles as applied to complex graphic design projects. Students strengthen skills in creating sophisticated compositions and typographic relationships, experiment with type as identity, emotion, and methodology. Letter grade only.

Subject: VC
Title: GRAPHIC DESIGN II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ART 135, ART 150, VC 201, and VC 202 with grades of C or better
Description: Studio course. An application of design and typographic principles, integrated into communication design projects of moderate and increasing complexity. Emphasis is on development of strong concepts that communicate persuasively and effectively across multiple platforms. Letter grade only.

Subject: STA
Title: PROBABILITY APPL IN ACT SCI
Description: Application of probability concepts and methods suitable for preparation for careers in the actuarial profession.  Use of random variables, distribution theory, specific probabilistic distributions, expected value, and related concepts in insurance models including deductibles, caps, premiums, and loss. Letter grade only.

Subject: GLG
Title: PRINCIPLES APPLIED GEOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Geology (BS) and Senior status or higher
Description: This course is designed to prepare students for the theory necessary to practice as a registered professional geologist in the State of Arizona. Letter grade only.

Subject: PRM
Title: FIELDWORK EXPERIENCE
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: (24 units from: PRM 220, PRM 252, PRM 275, PRM 346W, PRM 350, PRM 358, PRM 360, PRM 383, PRM 426, PRM 447)
Pre- or Corequisite: PRM 498C
Description: Individualized supervised field experience in an appropriate agency or organization. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: ART
Title: PROFESSIONAL PRACTICES IN ART
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Studio Art Major and Junior Status or higher
Description: This professional development course immerses students in a wide variety of practical applications toward art professions. Through lectures, demonstrations, and hands-on experience, students participate in the preparation for, application to, and administration of professional opportunities in the arts and related fields. From graduate-school to art careers and alternative opportunities, students leave this course with relevant portfolios and materials prepared to apply for post-graduation opportunities, and an informed future in the arts. Letter grade only.

Subject: ECO
Title: NATURAL RES & CLIMATE CHANGE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ECO 284, (MAT 121 or MAT 125 or higher) and Junior status or higher
Description: This course covers basic principles and methods economists have developed to characterize the efficient use of natural resources ranging from minerals and energy to fisheries, forestry, water, etc. The focus is on the problems commonly encountered in natural resource uses, root causes, policies, and how well different policies work. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: DEVELOPMENTAL BIOLOGY
Description: The cellular and molecular biology of animal development. Co-convened with BIO 451C. Letter grade only.

Subject: ID
Title: INTERIOR DESIGN III - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ID 362 with a grade of C or better; Honors Student Group
Description: Studio course. Facility planning. Advanced project problem solving, concentrating on facility planning of private and public interiors. Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: ASSMNT & INTRV CHALL BEHAVIOR
LongTitle: ASSESSMENT AND INTERVENTION OF CHALLENGING BEHAVIOR
Description: This course explores applied behavior analytic assessment and intervention to reduce challenging behavior and increase positive behavioral repertoires. Students will gain competence in selecting and applying principles of behavior across multiple areas of investigation. Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: TRAINING AND EVALUATION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (HA 345 and (Hotel & Restaurant Mgt (BS) or Human Resources in Hospitality Mgt (UCERT)); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Applications-based course in how to design, implement, and evaluate training using an instructional design model. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: HUMAN PHYSIOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (BIO 202 or BIO 325 or BIO 424); (CHM 360 or CHM 461); and (PHY 111 or PHY 161); Honors Student Group
Description: Physiology of major body systems studied at the metabolic, cellular, and systemic levels, emphasizing homeostatic mechanisms. Letter grade only.

Subject: CENE
Title: CRIT ISSUES IN DEEP FND DESIGN
LongTitle: CRITICAL ISSUES IN DEEP FOUNDATION DESIGN
Description: Advanced geotechnical engineering concepts related to deep foundation design and construction of large resilient infrastructure projects, including dewatering design, foundation failure analysis and risk assessment, slope stability analysis, and liquefaction analysis and its influence on foundation integrity. Emphasis will be on key issues leading to foundation failures and risk assessment involving building and bridge foundation design and construction. Letter grade only.

Subject: STA
Title: REGRESSION ANALYSIS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (STA 270 or STA 275) and MAT 136 with grades of C or better
Description: Simple linear, multiple linear, curvilinear, and multiple curvilinear regression models.  Simple, multiple, and partial correlation. Variable selection techniques. Letter grade only.

Subject: ARE
Title: CRITIQUING & WRITING ABOUT ART
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Admission to Art Secondary Education(BSED) or Studio Arts (BFA); Foundation English Requirement; and Junior Status) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Examines concepts and processes involved in critiquing and writing about art. Involves writing about and discussing art, inquiry, and context. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Contains an assessment that must be passed to student teach. Letter grade only.

Subject: HIS
Title: PRACTICUM IN THE SCHOOLS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: HIS 205 with a grade of B or better, HIS 206, and admission to the Professional Education Program (SECOND_ED Milestone). Open to History and Social Studies Education majors only.
Corequisite: HIS 429
Description: Field experience including a minimum 45 hours of work in a middle or high school classroom and the teaching of a lesson, guided reflections, observations, and collaborative learning design and implementation. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: FUNDAMENTALS NURSIN G PRACTC
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Admission to TBSN or CBSN
Corequisite:
TBSN: (NUR 322L, NUR 323, NUR 326, NUR 327, NUR 330, and NUR 355); or
CBSN: (NUR 322L, NUR 323, NUR 326, NUR 327, NUR 330, NUR 352, and NUR 355)
Description: Study of foundational concepts of nursing practice including the cognitive and psychomotor skill development needed to meet the basic human needs. Emphasis is placed on the development of student as a caring competent nurse at a beginning level. Provides knowledge of introductory skill development and health history and physical examinations skills essential for holistic practice. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: MECHANICS I - HONORS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: PHY 263 and (AST 265, PHY 265, or CS 122) and Honors Student Group
Pre- or Corequisite: MAT 239 or higher
Description: Review of Newtonian mechanics, drag forces, computer simulation of dynamical systems, systems of particles, oscillators, non-inertial frames of reference, Lagrangian mechanics, orbital dynamics.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MUS
Title: UNDERGRAD RESEARCH - HONORS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement; (MUS 222, MUS 241, MUS 242, and MUS 330W); Senior Status or higher; and Honors Student Group

All prerequisites must have grades of C or better
Description: Research paper seminar and term paper on a music related topic. Letter grade only. May be repeated for credit.

Subject: OTD
Title: RESIDENCY QUALIFICATION EXAM
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  OTD 708 (24 units) and Cumulative GPA 3.0
Description: The student will pass a competency requirement prior to the commencement of the doctoral residency component. The specific content and format of the competency requirement is determined by the program.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: SW
Title: ADVANCED FIELD EDUCATION I
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Admission to a graduate MSW program (SWMSW or SWADVMSW); (((SWMSW and (SW 595 or 30 units in a graduate MSW program)) or SWADVMSW)
Co requisite: SW 622
Description: Educationally focused enhanced internship in a practice setting with direct application of knowledge, values and skills of advanced generalist social work. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: SW
Title: PROGRAM & PRACTICE EVALUATION
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Admission to a graduate MSW program (SWMSW or SWADVMSW); (((SWMSW and (SW 595 or 30 units in a graduate MSW program)) or SWADVMSW)
Co requisite: SW 622
Description: Students will learn program and practice evaluation methods and apply these methods through course assignments. Letter grade only.

Subject: FW
Title: APPL MVMT ANLYS AND MOTOR LRNG
LongTitle: APPLIED MOVEMENT ANALYSIS AND MOTOR LEARNING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 192, BIO 201, BIO 201L, and HS 200 with grades of C or better in each
Description: Foundational components of movement analysis and motor learning specific to the improvement of teaching and learning in the physical education domain. Letter grade only.

Subject: INF
Title: ADV SURVEY ECOINF TOOLS
Prerequisites: Admission to Informatics & Computing PhD, Informatics MS, Computational and Applied Data Science MS, Computer Science MS, Computer Science and Engineering MEng, or Electrical Engineering MEng
Description: This course surveys data collection tools and products used by ecoinformaticists, along with introductions to the organizations and professionals that build and utilize those tools and products; builds applied skill in using those tools for the purpose of conducting graduate research. Co-convened with INF 450. Letter grade only.

Subject: CPP
Title: CLINICAL PSYCH PRACTICUM IV
Description: Supervised advanced clinical field experience at an assigned clinical training site, and weekly practicum seminar meetings on campus. A focus on assessment issues or psychotherapy, or both assessment and intervention will be intermixed over the two years of practicum in preparation for a year-long clinical internship. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for a maximum of 6 units.

Subject: CST
Title: SENIOR SEM COMM STDS - HONORS
LongTitle: SENIOR SEMINAR IN COMMUNICATION STUDIES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CST 271, CST 300W; 15 hours of CST coursework; Senior Status; Honors Student Group
Description: Surveys recent research and conceptual trends in communication studies. During the course, you prepare a substantive research project and publicly present and defend your conclusions. Letter grade only.

Subject: ID
Title: FIELD WORK EXPERIENCE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ID 322 and Senior Status or higher and Cumulative GPA greater than 1.99
Description: Internship. Supervised field experience in an appropriate agency or organization.  Department consent required.  Pass-fail only.  May be repeated for credit.

Subject: SITA
Title: II DECAMERON - BIRTH/NOVELLA
LongTitle: II DECAMERON - BIRTH OF THE NOVELLA
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Siena Study Abroad Student Group
Description: Analysis of the concepts, style, and personality of Boccaccio who embodies the entire epoch of contradictions, natural wit, and behavior.  Taught in Italian.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SART
Title: ART AND PHOTOGRAPHY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Siena Study Abroad Student Group
Description: Image making with photography in order to discuss, express, and communicate views and interests in the world we live in.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SITA
Title: SURVEY OF ITALY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Siena Study Abroad Student Group
Description: Survey of Italy and its regions including geographic characteristics, food, cities, artistic attractions, monuments, events, fashion brands, writers, and singers.  Taught in Italian.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SITA
Title: ITALIAN SIGN LANGUAGE II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Siena Study Abroad Student Group
Description: Examination of the history, culture, and education of the Italian Deaf Community from a multidisciplinary perspective.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SITA
Title: HISTORY OF SIENA
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Siena Study Abroad Student Group
Description: Introduction to Italian medieval and modern history through an in-depth analysis of Sienas economic-social structures and everyday life.  Taught in Italian.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SART
Title: PAINTING I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Siena Study Abroad Student Group
Description: Introduction to still-life and representational painting using basic painting materials and techniques.  Focus is on painting from direct observation.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SARH
Title: RENAISSANCE/SIENA & FLORENCE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Siena Study Abroad Student Group
Description: An examination of the dramatic cultural changes and stylistic developments from the 15th to the first one-half of the 16th centuries viewed in the context of central Italian cities.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SW
Title: SOCIAL WORK RESEARCH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (SW 220 and Social Work Milestone) or International Exchange Student Group  Corequisite:  SW 321
Description: Problem formulation, research design, data collection and analysis, and the social context of social work research, including the systematic evaluation of practice. Letter grade only.

Subject: PADM
Title: PUB POLICY FORMATION & STRAT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: PADM 301 and Junior Status or higher
Description: In this course students will develop an understanding of how public policy is formed and the strategies utilized in the policy-making process.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EGR
Title: INTRO TO EGR DESIGN - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Honors Student Group and (MAT 125 or higher with a grade of C or better or MP70)
Description: Introduces the design process, problem-solving techniques, teaming skills, oral and written communication skills, and tools for success in academic and professional careers. Multiple hands-on projects. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: PRINCIPLES OF MACROECONOMICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 114 or higher with a grade of C or better or Math Placement Test Results (ALEKS/MATHA 50+; MATHC 50+; PLACE 55+)
Description: Measurement of national economic variables; determination of output, income, employment, and price levels through aggregate supply and demand analysis; business cycles, fiscal and monetary policy, global implications of international trade and production.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: PRINCIPLES OF MICROECONOMICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 114 or higher with a grade of C or better or Math Placement Test Results (ALEKS/MATHA 50+; MATHC 50+; PLACE 55+)
