Description: A systematic investigation of the operation of a market economy ; supply and demand; consumer behavior; production and costs; determination of prices in various product and resource markets; causes and consequences of income distribution, international economics.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NAUY
Title: DYNAMIC ORGANIZATIONS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement and academic advisor approval
Description: Explores organizational dynamics, emphasizing factors contributing to extraordinary success. Incorporates theoretical perspectives and case studies. Department consent required. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: COMPLEX VARIABLES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  MAT 238 with a grade of C or better and Honors Student Group
Description: Fundamental theory and applications of complex variables and analytic functions with emphasis on integration, power series, and the calculus of residues.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: FAMILY PRIMARY HEALTH CARE III
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  NUR 662 and NUR 663 
Corequisite:  NUR 665
Description: This course provides the graduate student with advanced theoretical knowledge in the assessment and management of selected acute, chronic and complex health conditions across the lifespan.  Letter grade only.

Subject: REL
Title: RECENT TRENDS IN RELIGION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Sophomore Status or higher); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Topical study of selected religious trends that significantly affect the world today.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: REL
Title: BUDDHIST SCRIPTURES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Sophomore Status or higher); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Examines the major themes and ideas of Buddhist scriptures in the historical context of their origin. Letter grade only.

Subject: CCJ
Title: LAW IN AMERICA
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Sophomore Status or higher); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Theoretical study of sources of law; issues in the implementation of law; assessment of effects of law in American society. Letter grade only.

Subject: REL
Title: HINDU SCRIPTURES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Sophomore Status or higher); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: This course provides an in-depth examination of Hindu scripture, from the early Vedic Period (~ 1500 BCE) through the great epics, (the Mahabharata and the Ramayana, the Puranas, the Tantras) and the non-sectarian devotional literature by surveying its features according to the categories of comparative religion, and attending to its historical development and internal diversity. Letter grade only.

Subject: ID
Title: ARCHITECTURAL RENDERING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ID 121 and ID 123 with grades of C or better
Description: Studio course. Use of free-hand and digital rendering tools, including Photoshop, Illustrator and Sketch-Up as appropriate to Interior Design. Letter grade only.

Subject: GER
Title: FIRST YEAR GERMAN
Description: Continuation of GER 101. Letter grade only.

Subject: CCJ
Title: PERSPECTIVES ON JUSTICE POLICY
Description: This course provides a critical exploration of the contours of contemporary justice policy focused on understanding the complexities and realities of justice policy in a changing and interconnected world.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CCJ
Title: CORRECTIONS & SOCIAL CONTROL
Description: This course provides an examination of issues facing correctional systems both inside and outside the United States. Special attention is given to theories of punishment, the regulation and management of criminal offenders, the use of the correctional system as a broader mechanism of social control and an assessment of effective alternatives to the mass incarceration policies that currently predominate U.S. correctional policy. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: CELLULAR AND MOLECULAR BIOLOGY
Description: Organization and function of biological molecules, cell diversity, cell structure and function, and cell interaction and differentiation. Letter grade only. Recommended: BIO 340.

Subject: AST
Title: INTRO TO OBSERVATIONAL ASTRNMY
Description: Lab to accompany AST 180. Astronomical observations and experiments. Use of telescope is stressed. Letter grade only.

Subject: DIS
Title: ASSESSMENT EFFECTIVE PRACTICES
LongTitle: ASSESSMENT AND EFFECTIVE PRACTICES IN ASSISTIVE TECHNOLOGY
Description: Provides students with knowledge and skills in assessing the need for assistive technology and reviews current effective practices for providing assistive technology services.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EDL
Title: DISSERTATION
Description: Individualized directed research, writing, and oral defense of selected dissertation topic.  Department consent required.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: GSP
Title: ENTERPRISE GEODATABASES
Description: Introduces the design, development, and management of enterprise geodatabases.  Students build geodatabases using real-world data and develop municipal and local government GIS applications. Software includes Oracle and ArcSDE, ArcCatalog and ArcMap. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: GSP
Title: PROGRAMMING FOR GIS
Description: Introduction to computer programming principles and their application in a GIS environment. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: TOPICS IN COMBINATORICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to Mathematics MS or (MAT 226, MAT 316, and MAT 411 with grades of C or better)
Description: Topics in enumerative, algebraic, and geometric combinatorics, chosen at instructors discretion; may include advanced counting techniques, graph theory, combinatorial designs, matroids, and error-correcting codes. Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: BEVERAGE PRODUCTION & SERVICE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (HA 240 and Hotel and Restaurant Management (BS)); Event Management & MICE Certificate; or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Introduces the history of the beverage industry and the production and classification of alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages. The sales, service, and control system of the beverages are also discussed. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: VIROLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (BIO 344 and BIO 350) and Honors Student Group
Description: Biophysical, genetic and biochemical characteristics of mammalian and microbial viruses. Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: ADMIN & SUPRVN READING PRGMS
LongTitle: ADMINISTRATION AND SUPERVISION OF READING PROGRAMS
Description: Creation, maintenance, and evaluation of reading programs for kindergarten through adult.  Administration, staffing, politics, training, evaluating strengths and weaknesses, finances, and leadership within reading programs.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: AST
Title: INTRODUCTION TO ASTROPHYSICS
Description: Calculus-based introductory astrophysics course covering solar system mechanics, overview of planets, electromagnetic radiation, telescopes, stellar evolution, galactic structure, interstellar medium, galaxies, and cosmology. Letter grade only.

Subject: PRM
Title: NATURAL RESOURCES PROTECTION
Description: This course introduces Federal regulations which provide protection of Americas natural resources. Evolution and applicability of environmental laws regulating consumptive and non-consumptive uses of plants, wildlife and fish are discussed. Letter grade only.

Subject: POS
Title: TOPICS IN PUBLIC ADMINISTRATN
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Sophomore status or higher); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Analyzes selected contemporary public administration issues in national and international politics.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit with different content.

Subject: ENV
Title: ENVIRON CHALLENGES & SOLUTIONS
Description: Studies the relations between humans and the environment, such as life (e.g., animals, plants) and ecosystems, natural resources, and climate change. Students will gain a scientific understanding of the natural world around them; examine effects of current events, policies, and personal lifestyle on the environment; and explore solutions to current environmental problems. Letter grade only.

Subject: MUS
Title: MUSIC THEORY II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  MUS 121 w/ grade of C or better
Description: Continued study of diatonic harmony; secondary functions; common-chord modulation; and binary and ternary forms. Note that MUS 122 is normally taken concurrently with MUS 132. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHI
Title: DECISION THEORY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (MAT 114 or Math Placement 55) and 3 units of PHI coursework
Description: Theory of rational decision-making, applications of the theory to ethics and economics, and discussion of philosophical problems arising from the constructs employed by the theory (e.g., probability and utility).  Letter grade only.

Subject: FOR
Title: DENDROECOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Graduate status or Senior undergraduate status
Description: Concepts and methods of dendroecology - the application of dated tree-ring information to ecological investigations.  Principles of dendrochronology, cross-dating, and application to climate, hydrology, insect outbreaks, past forest conditions.  Letter grade only.

Subject: GLG
Title: ADVANCED STRUCTURAL GEOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Graduate status in the School of Earth and Sustainability
Description: Advanced study of deformation of Earths crust. Stress and strain theory, deformation mechanisms, geometry, kinematics, and dynamics of orogenic belts. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: GLG
Title: REGIONAL TECTONICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Graduate status in the School of Earth and Sustainability
Description: Geologic principles, plate tectonic theory applied to the interpretation of geologic history on a regional scale. Lecture and field trips. Letter grade only.

Subject: EES
Title: TOPICS EARTH & ENV SCI & POL
LongTitle: TOPICS IN EARTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCE AND POLICY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Graduate status in the School of Earth and Sustainability
Description: Introduction to research and grant-proposal writing and to local and global geologic, environmental, and policy issues. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: EES
Title: RESEARCH METHODS
LongTitle: RESEARCH METHODS IN EARTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Graduate status in the School of Earth and Sustainability
Description: Continued development of research, writing, and presentation skills for graduate studies, leading to successful presentation of a MS thesis proposal or PhD dissertation chapter. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: CM
Title: CM INTEGRATED LAB II
Description: Development and management of information associated with a construction project including the development of drawings, details, and digital models. Letter grade only.

Subject: CM
Title: SAFETY AND RISK MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  CM 200L with a grade of C or better
Description: Examination of safety and risk management concepts that apply to the construction industry with an emphasis on OSHA regulations. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: CMH COUNSELING PRACTICUM
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: EPS 660, EPS 670, and (Admission to Clinical Mental Health Counseling (MA), Counseling-School Counseling (MEd), or Combined Counseling/School Psychology (PhD))

Corequisite: EPS 661 or Admission to College & Career Readiness GCRT
Description: Supervised, on-campus counseling practicum experiences, emphasizing interaction with individuals seeking service from counselors. One hundred clock hours minimum experience. This course contains an assessment that must be passed to be eligible to register for the internship. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: ART
Title: FIGURE DRAWING I
Description: Concentrated basic study in drawing the nude figure, with emphasis on perceptual skills using various media. 1 hr. lecture, 4 hrs. studio. Letter grade only. May be repeated for a maximum of 6 units.

Subject: AIS
Title: INDIGENIZING RESEARCH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  One 200- or 300-level AIS-Applied Indigenous Studies course
Description: The course introduces students to the ideological, political, and practical issues involved in managing and conducting research with indigenous communities. Letter grade only.

Subject: ART
Title: EXHIB MKG AS ARTISTIC MEDIUM
Description: The course explores the experimental exhibition-making process as an artistic medium. Students will understand the rise of the exhibition as a critical form and artistic medium and how it relates to site-specificity. Each student will produce a spatial setting reflecting the social inscription of objects, images, and (inter)actions. Letter grade only.

Subject: ANT
Title: CULTURAL ANTHROPOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ANT 102 and Admission to Anthropology major or minor
Description: Surveys the major theoretical, methodological, and empirical foundations of cultural anthropology. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: STRATEGIC MANAGEMENT
Description: The role of the general manager in dealing with strategic business challenges and providing leadership to the organization; integration of functional areas, policy formulation and implementation, multinational and ethical issues. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: BUSINESS PLAN DEVELOPMENT
Description: Design, development, and evaluation of a business plan for existing and new businesses. Includes consideration of marketing, industry, financial, management, competitive, and strategic issues relating to the feasibility and operation of new and ongoing businesses. Letter grade only.

Subject: DH
Title: CLINIC IV ORAL HLTH CAPSTONE
LongTitle: CLINIC IV ORAL HEALTH OUTCOMES CAPSTONE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  DH 411 with a grade of C or better
Description: This capstone course integrates dental hygiene sciences, clinical experience, advanced procedures and best evidence in standards of care to make and evaluate clinical oral health decisions, culminating in a written paper and oral presentation of a case study patient. 2 hrs. lecture; 12 hrs. clinic. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: METHODS OF ANALYTICAL PHYSICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  (PHY 265 or CS 122)  Pre- or Corequisite: MAT 239 or higher
Description: Applications of mathematical methods of physics.  Letter grade only.

Subject: HS
Title: HUMAN DISEASES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite or Corequisite:  HS 200 with a grade of C or better
Description: Overview of acute and chronic diseases, how these diseases affect the human body, and actions one might take to reduce the risk. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: STUDENT AFFAIRS PRACTICUM
Description: This course is a supervised Student Affairs practicum-internship experience emphasizing interaction with individuals and groups seeking student support services.  Pass/Fail only.

Subject: PHO
Title: ADVANCED PHOTOGRAPHY
Description: Advanced exposure and image manipulation techniques. Emphasizes compositing raw format images. Digital-SLR with dedicated flash required. Letter grade only.

Subject: VC
Title: ANIMATION III
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (Animation Milestone or Motion Design Milestone); VC 365 with a grade of C or better
Description: Studio course. Students will build on the skills learned in the 300-level animation classes, and produce an ambitious animation film. They will be expected to exhibit advanced technique, storytelling and content, and work to develop as creative artists through self-critique. Time management and planning skills for projects in animation are emphasized. Letter grade only.

Subject: MGBA
Title: GLOBAL LEADERSHIP
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to MGBA program, MGBA 550, and MGBA 560
Description: This course examines the variety of approaches to leadership across the globe, as well as significant similarities. Issues of history, culture, business practices, and leadership will be examined. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: SOLID STATE PHYSICS II
Description: This course involves the study of solids from the perspective of the underlying microscopic structure of rigid matter and will focus upon materials with nanoscale dimensions.  Co-convened with PHY 582.  Letter grade only.

Subject: SW
Title: DIR PRCT MENT & BEH HLTH W/CHI
LongTitle: DIRECT PRACTICE IN MENTAL & BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WITH CHILDREN AND ADOLESCENTS
Prerequisites:
Prerequiste: Admission to MSADVMSW or ((admission to SWMSW and (SW 595 or 30 units in MSW program)) 
Corequisite: SW 636
Description: This course will explore mental health issues as they apply to children and adolescents. Students will learn how to engage, assess, and treat and evaluate diverse client systems in diverse professional settings. Letter grade only.

Subject: CCJ
Title: INVESTIGATING DIFFERENCE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ((CCJ 101 and CCJ 250) and (9 hours of CCJ-Criminology & Criminal Justice coursework); Foundation English Requirement; and (Junior status or above)); or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Explores the development and effects of categories of difference and inequality on patterns of crime and victimization, and the meaning and practice of justice. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENV
Title: WATER RESOURCES POLICY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Senior status or instructor consent
Description: This course examines water resources policy and management through an international framework. Through a combination of lectures, readings materials, guest lectures, and field trips this class draws on interdisciplinary perspectives to examine case studies and analyze institutions from across the U.S. and abroad. Co-convened with ENV 525. Letter grade only.

Subject: TH
Title: CONTEMPORARY ACTING TECHNIQUES
Description: Practice of basic techniques in scenes from realistic dramatic literature emphasizing individual and ensemble performance.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ID
Title: INTERIOR DESIGN IV - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ID 461 and Honors Student Group
Description: Studio course. Comprehensive large-scale projects in commercial and institutional interiors. Letter grade only.

Subject: JUS
Title: FINAL PROJECT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: 26 units from the MOL plan
Description: Individualized final or capstone project. Letter grade only.

Subject: BSCI
Title: BUILDING PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BSCI 501 or CENE 503 with a grade of B or better
Description: This course is an intermediate examination of contemporary building science topics and the means and methods currently used in practice to deliver performance-based projects. Cross-listed with CENE 513. Letter grade only.

Subject: ANT
Title: PRE-THESIS SEMINAR
Description: Development of skills and knowledge necessary for planning, organizing, and carrying out thesis research in anthropology. Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: UNIVERSITY PHYSICS II LAB
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  PHY 161 or PHY 171  and Prerequisite or Corequisite: MAT 137 or higher and PHY 262
Description: Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: ADV SEMINAR IN CURR PROBLEMS
Description: Letter grade only.

Subject: FOR
Title: FOREST MEASUREMENTS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: MAT 125 or higher 
Pre- or Corequisite: STA 270
Description: Fundamentals of measuring forest ecosystems and resources, land surveying, mapping, and orienteering.  2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: COMPUTER APPS IN LINGUISTICS
Description: Variable-content course focusing on either computer techniques for computer-assisted language learning or programming techniques for corpus linguistics.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: ENG
Title: PRAGMATICS
Description: Introduces theory and techniques for the pragmatic analysis of spoken and written language.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: ASSMT FOR SECND LANG SKILLS
Description: Studies principles and practices of second language assessment.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: MICROBIOLOGY
Description: Fundamental microbiology emphasizing structure and function of organisms, aspects of microbial diversity, pathogenic microorganisms, disease mechanisms and immune response.  For students majoring in microbiology and those in the health professions.  Recommended:  CHM 130 or CHM 151.  Letter grade only.

Subject: OTD
Title: EDUCATIONAL STRATEGIES & LRNG
LongTitle: EDUCATIONAL STRATEGIES AND LEARNING IN HEALTHCARE AND ACADEMIC SETTINGS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: OTD 600, OTD 602, OTD 620, OTD 621, and OTD 651
Description: One of the core principles in occupational therapy delivery, the teaching-learning process is studied and practiced. Activity analysis and learning strategies across a variety of service delivery contexts to promote performance, engagement and behavioral change is considered.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NTS
Title: NUTR FOR SP PRF & WLL - HONORS
LongTitle: NUTRITION FOR SPORT PERFORMANCE & WELLNESS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (BIO 201, BIO 201L, BIO 202, BIO 202L, and NTS 135 with grades of C or better in each); Honors Student Group
Description: This course is a required course in the Nutrition and Foods major and also the Nutrition and Foods minor. This course provides the link between basic nutrition and food planning content and the connection with physical activity and physical performance. It will explore the unique energy needs of and nutritional considerations for those participating and many different athletic events. The course will address the special nutritional and training needs of athletes and individuals who are physically active. Major topics include the function and sources of each of the nutrients, fueling for pre exercise, during exercise and after exercise, specific meal planning, nutrient needs for specific athletes and wellness lifestyles, and more. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: MTHDS TEACHG LIT SECNDRY CLSRM
LongTitle: METHODS OF TEACHING LITERATURE IN THE SECONDARY CLASSROOM
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement and 9 additional units of ENG-English coursework
Description: Materials, planning techniques, classroom methods, and evaluative devices specific to the teaching of literature in the secondary school. Co-convenes with ENG 580.  Instructors consent required. This course contains an assessment that must be successfully completed in order to register for student teaching. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: INTRO TO NUMERICAL ANALYSIS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 239 with a grade of C or better and (ISM 220 or CS 122 or CS 126 or EE 222)
Description: Algorithms, computational errors, single variable equations, curve fitting, interpolation, numerical differentiation and integration, numerical solutions of differential equations, and linear systems of equations. Includes computer lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: MICROBIAL ECOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 205, 220 or permission of instructor
Description: Population, community, and ecosystem ecology of microorganisms emphasizing interactions with plants and animals and the roles of microorganisms in ecosystem processes. Co-convenes with BIO 471C. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: STATISTICS FOR MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation Math Requirement with a grade of C or better
Description: Covers the nature of probability and statistics and their use in managerial decision making, including the estimation of population parameters, hypothesis testing, regression, correlation, analysis of variance and nonparametric methods.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: BUSINESS STATS & INFO SYSTEMS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation Math Requirement with a grade of C or better
Description: Covers the nature of probability and statistics and their use in managerial decision making, including the estimation of population parameters, hypothesis testing, regression, correlation, analysis of variance and nonparametric methods. Information is organized using databases, spreadsheets, word and information processing, and presentation graphics. Letter grade only.

Subject: ART
Title: COLOR PRINTMAKING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Any 100- or 200-level printmaking course (ART 141, ART 142, ART 241, or ART 242)
Description: Studio course. Explores and incorporates color in printing processes. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 hours of credit.

Subject: HS
Title: PRINCIPLES OF EPIDEMIOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (HS 300 with grade of C or better), (Health Sci: Public Health Milestone or BS HS: Allied Health plans or BAS HS plan or Minor in Health Education); and Foundation Math Requirement with a grade of C or better
Description: Concepts and methods for analyzing the spread and control of disease: trends in solving community health problems. Letter grade only.

Subject: FOR
Title: FOREST PATHOLOGY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  One FOR or BIO or ENV course and Junior status
Description: Important forest tree diseases, their identification, ecology, and management. Co-convened with FOR 552. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: FOR
Title: FOREST HEALTH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  One FOR or BIO or ENV course and Junior status
Description: Modern concepts of maintaining tree health and the relationships between tree and forest health.  Agents and processes of decline in forest ecosystems.  Co-convenes with FOR 554.  Letter grade only.

Subject: FOR
Title: FOREST INSECTS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  One FOR or BIO or ENV course and Junior status
Description: Identification, biology, population dynamics, ecological role, and management of forest insects in western forests.  Co-convenes with FOR 553.  Letter grade only.

Subject: AS
Title: HERITAGE & VALUE II
Description: Continuation of AS 101. This a survey course designed to introduce students to the Department of the Air Force (DAF) and provides an overview of the basic characteristics, missions, communications and organization of the Air and Space Forces. Letter grade only.

Subject: CCJ
Title: INTERNSHIP
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CCJ-Criminology & Criminal Justice GPA of 3.0 or better
Description: Supervised field experience in an appropriate agency or organization. Up to 6 hours may be applied to criminal justice elective requirements. Department consent required. Pass/Fail only. May be repeated for a maximum of 12 units.

Subject: CST
Title: ARGUMENTATION AND DEBATE
Description: Decision making through argumentation, with special emphasis on the analysis of issues, reasoning, and refutation.  Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: QUALITATIVE METHODS & ANALYSIS
Description: Characteristics, applications, and implications of qualitative research methods; comparison of positivist and interpretive forms of qualitative research.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CYB
Title: NETWORK SECURITY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 212, CYB 136, and MAT 226 with grades of C or better in each
Description: An overview of network security and related topics including threat classification, vulnerabilities as they manifest in TCP/IP, authentication/authorization, access control, log/traffic monitoring, reputation-based security, and secure protocols (SSH, TLS, DNSSEC). Co-convened with CYB 504. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: GEN ORGANIC CHEMISTRY II REC
Description: Optional recitation section covering problem-solving techniques in organic chemistry.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: PHY
Title: QUANTUM MECHANICS
Description: Wave mechanics, Schrodingers equation, barrier problems, harmonic oscillator, hydrogen atom and matrix methods. Co-convened with APMS 571. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: INTRO TO ABSTRACT ALGEBRA II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 411 with a grade of C or better
Description: Topics selected from the theory and applications of abstract algebra; groups, rings, fields. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: MTHDS TCHG SEC SCH MATH II
LongTitle: METHODS OF TEACHING SECONDARY SCHOOL MATHEMATICS II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: TSM 101, TSM 201, MAT 401, and STA 275 with grades of C or better
Description: This course covers content, pedagogical and technological trends and issues related to teaching and learning probability/statistics, calculus, and mathematical and statistical modeling in grades seven through twelve. Emphases are on quantitative literacy; visualizing and summarizing data; statistical inference; probability; applied statistical problems; calculation of instantaneous rates of change; and summation of infinitely many small factors to determine some whole. Letter grade only.

Subject: LAN
Title: CAPSTONE SEMINAR
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: FRE 304W, GER 304W, JPN 304W, SPA 304W or SPA 312W
Description: BA in Modern Languages capstone course targeting critical thinking and effective writing.  Students choose their individual project in consultation with the instructor of the course and their target-language advisor.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MS
Title: LEADERSHIP LAB
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Completion of documentation by first class to determine eligibility for enrollment  Corequisite:  MS 101 and MS 150
Description: Military leadership and small unit tactics.  Develops leadership abilities through practical application and challenging outdoor activities.  Letter grade only.

Subject: DH
Title: ORAL MED & PHARMACOLOGY II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  DH 300 with grade of C or better
Description: Overview of oral medicine and pharmacology with emphasis on application to dental hygiene care. Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: PROGRAMMING FOR ENG & SCI
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  MAT 108 or higher with a grade of C or better; or Math Placement Test Results (ALEKS/MATHA 50+; MATHC 50+; PLACE 55+)
Corequisite:  CS 122L
Description: Introduces computer programming for engineers, scientists, and math majors. Emphasizes problem solving, algorithms, and structured programming. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: GENERAL PATHOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BIO 202, BIO 205 and Honors Student Group
Description: Surveys disease processes affecting body systems; cell death and inflammation; emphasizes altered physiological mechanisms in cells and organ systems.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CM
Title: CM INTEGRATED LAB III
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  CM 300L and CM 329 with grades of C or better
Description: Development of project management skills related to construction projects including schedule and cost control, safety and risk management, quality assurance, project coordination and leadership. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: NSG CARE CHILD BEARING REARING
LongTitle: NURSING CARE OF CHILD-BEARING AND CHILD-REARING FAMILIES
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  NUR 334, NUR 334L, NUR 340 
Corequisite:  NUR 339, NUR 441, NUR 450C, NUR 408
Description: This course examines transitions of individuals and families across the life span, emphasizing childbearing processes, childhood experiences, and the effects of illness on childbearing women and children.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ME
Title: MATERIALS SCIENCE
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: CHM 151
Pre- or Corequisite:  PHY 262
Prerequisites must have grades of C or better
Description: Relationships between the microstructure of materials and their physical and engineering properties. Basic metallurgy/ceramic/polymer science; processing, structure, properties, and performance of engineering materials. Letter grade only.

Subject: GLG
Title: INT GEOLOGIC FIELD METHODS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: EES 240  
Pre- or Corequisite: GLG 315 and GLG 324
Description: This course is designed to develop and expand the geologic field skills learned in EES 240 and apply them to more complex mapping areas. We will emphasize field mapping and interpretation of sedimentary, igneous, and metamorphic rocks, and use cross cutting relationships and geologic structures  Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: MOBILE APPLICATION DEVELOPMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 249 with a grade of C or better
Description: Mobile devices have enabled perhaps the greatest expansion of popular computing access since the advent of the internet itself, and have created a large and vibrant new sector in the software development economy. At the same time, programming mobile devices has introduced a range of new challenges for the software developer, ranging from the limited amount of real estate on mobile screens, limited input and interaction techniques, unstable or intermittent network access, and significant power consumption limits. This elective course provides those students interested in working in this exciting sector with hands-on training in mobile app development principles, frameworks, and concepts. It is complementary to the subsequent CS477 Advanced Interfaces course, which examines user interface design and usability evaluation in more depth. Letter grade only.

Subject: SE
Title: SOFTWARE TESTING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 249 with a grade of C or better
Description: Intensive study of the underlying concepts, algorithms, and software tooling associated with testing of embedded and low-level software systems, in for particular safety-critical or security-critical contexts. Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: VIRTUAL WORLDS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 249 with a grade of C or better
Description: Explores the data modeling, visualization, and simulation of abstract environments.  Topics include data organization, freeform and tile-based 2D and 3D graphics techniques, collision physics, AI, and networked game infrastructures. Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: 3D GAME DEVELOPMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 249 with a grade of C or better
Description: Introduces the concepts and production pipelines associated with 3D game development. Topics include affine transformations, polygonal models, textures, environments, animation, positional sound, physics, and visual effects. Small teams will create game prototypes that synthesize concepts into practice. Letter grade only.

Subject: CYB
Title: SECURE DESIGN II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CYB 126 with a grade of C or better
Description: Intermediate topics related to computer software and digital hardware and defensive design strategies. Topics include secure design as applied to object-oriented programming, contractual design, and provable computing. Letter grade only.

Subject: POR
Title: SECOND YEAR PORTUGUESE
Description: Portuguese 212 is a continuing accelerated Portuguese language and Luso-Brazilian culture course, with an emphasis on Brazil, which serves as a crossroads between European (e.g., Portugal), African, and Indigenous cultures. This course aims to increase students levels of proficiency via the everyday use of the target language. By continuous practice with the target language, it is expected that students will be able to spontaneously create with the language, initiate, sustain and close intermediate-level exchanges, ask and answer questions and possess the skills necessary to circumlocute when faced with a more difficult communicative task. Additionally, students will be able to meet intermediate practical writing needs by creating compositions, descriptive narratives and, crucially, will determine the message, the primary thesis, and/or the socio-cultural context of simple expository and narrative texts (written and oral). Since instruction is conducted entirely in Portuguese, studen

Subject: ECI
Title: STUDENT TEACHING REFLECTION
Description: The seminar provides a forum for final reflection on program of study coursework and how it applies within the directed elementary Student Teaching experience.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: CORE INTRODUCTORY SEMINAR
Description: This course fosters the foundation for all subsequent coursework.  It initiates the theme of "Community" through a set of common readings and is linked to the capstone course to ensure program continuity.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: SCI/SOC STUDIES METHODS ELMTRY
Description: Course offers future educators a practical understanding of an integrated curricular approach by infusing inquiry-based science methods with social studies methods using a thematic approach.  This course contains an assessment that must be passed to be eligible to register for student teaching.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ECI
Title: ELEMTRY CURRICULUM AND ASSESMT
Description: Students will explore various theoretical and methodological aspects of elementary school curriculum, as well as the role of assessment in the teaching and learning process. Curriculum content and organization, as well as instructional design, methods and materials for grades K-8. Candidates develop skills, knowledge and dispositions necessary for teaching students with exceptionalities. Professional responsibility and ethical conduct are integrated into this course. This course contains an assessment that must be passed to be eligible to register for Contains an assessment that must be successfully completed in order to move toward student teaching. Letter grade only.

Subject: BME
Title: SEI IN EARLY CHILD SETTINGS
LongTitle: STRUCTURED ENGLISH IMMERSION IN EARLY CHILDHOOD SETTINGS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Early Childhood Milestone (BSED or BAS) and Foundation English Requirement
Description: The teaching of content in English to English learners in a Pre K-3 setting using appropriate approaches, methods and techniques. This course contains an assessment that must be successfully completed in order to register for student teaching. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: DISSERTATION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to Educational Psychology (PhD) program
Description: Individualized directed research, writing, and oral defense of selected dissertation topic.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: JLS
Title: PLACE-BASED REPORTING TOPICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: JLS 231, Foundation English Requirement, and (Junior Status or higher)
Description: Students learn the techniques, research, and function of investigative and feature storytelling as they work on larger reporting projects. This course fulfills NAUs junior-level writing requirement. Letter grade only. May be taken for up to 9 units of credit with different topics.

Subject: SW
Title: GENERALIST PRACTICE III
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  SW 321 and Social Work Milestone  
Corequisite:  SW 422 
Pre- or Corequisite:  SW 427
Description: Practice principles applied to intervention with macro-level client systems, including community and organizational change primarily in rural localities.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: PLANTS AND CLIMATE
Description: Analyzes plant physiological functions, including photosynthesis, transpiration, respiration, and energy balance, and interactions with the past and current climate system.  3 hours lecture.  Recommended:  CHM 360, BIO 284.  Letter grade only.

Subject: GSP
Title: LAND USE PLANNING
Description: Examines concepts, definitions, and the legal context and practice of land use planning and public controls with emphasis on zoning, subdivision controls, growth management, sustainability, and comprehensive plan implementation. Letter grade only.

Subject: FOR
Title: ECOL RESTORATION - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  One FOR or BIO or ENV course and Honors Student Group
Description: Principles and applications of ecology related to restoring natural structure, function, and composition of ecosystems. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: LAB SAFETY AND SUPERVISION
Description: Introduces lab safety, use and disposal of hazardous chemicals, emergency procedures, and lab supervision.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 2 units of credit.

Subject: FRE
Title: SECOND YEAR FRENCH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (FRE 102 or FRE 105), or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Continuation of FRE 101 and 102. Letter grade only.

Subject: STA
Title: MATHEMATICAL STATISTICS II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: STA 673 with grade of C or better
Description: Rigorous mathematical treatment of the theory of statistics; estimation, tests of hypotheses, selected topics from linear models and analysis of variance.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: PHYSICAL OPTICS
Description: Electromagnetic theory, geometrical optics, waves, polarization, interference, and diffraction.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: MODERN PHYSICS
Description: Special relativity, atomic and nuclear physics, quantum mechanics.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: ABSTRACT ALGEBRA II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: MAT 511 with grade of C or better
Description: Continuation of MAT 511.  Rings and modules, field extensions and Galois theory, and advanced topics at instructors discretion.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BSC
Title: GENETICS LAB
Description: This course will present some of the fundamental experiments and techniques associated in the field of genetics. Classical genetic experiments will involve Population genetics and Mendelian crosses. Molecular genetic experiments will involve DNA extraction, polymerase chain reaction (PCR), primer design, gel electrophoresis, and basic laboratory techniques. Many of these techniques will be taught from a research-oriented approach. Letter grade only.

Subject: SW
Title: SOCIAL WK ASSESSMENT - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: [Human Biology cognate: ((BIO 201 and BIO 201L), BIO 310, HS 300, or SW 310)]; PSY 240 and SW 321; Social Work Milestone; Honors Student Group
Description: Integrative course that links and applies human behavior theory in biological, psychological, social, cultural, and environmental contexts to assessment in generalist social work practice. Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: ETHICS IN ABA/PBS
LongTitle: ETHICS IN APPLIED BEHAVIOR ANALYSIS AND POSITIVE BEHAVIOR SUPPORT
Description: Students will examine the BACB Guidelines for Responsible Conduct in the context of: (a) responsible conduct; (b) responsibility towards clients, (c) assessing behavior, (d) providing treatment, (e) acting as a teacher/supervisor, (f) conduct in their workplace, (g) responsibility to the field of behavior analysis, (h) responsibility to colleagues, (i) responsibility to society, and (j) research.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: ADVANCED POSITIVE BEHAV SUPPRT
LongTitle: ADVANCED APPLICATIONS IN POSITIVE BEHAVIOR SUPPORT
Description: This course builds on students knowledge of PBS through problem-based learning.  Students work in teams where they will solve several case examples of children/adults with disabilities and challenging behavior. Throughout the course, new content will be taught via online readings and texts. Students must develop new functional behavioral assessment (FBA) tools, interpret difficult assessments, and design advanced PBS plans.  Letter grade only.

Subject: AS
Title: AIR FORCE PHYSICAL TRNG VI
LongTitle: AIR FORCE PHYSICAL TRAINING COURSE VI
Description: Opportunity for NAU students who meet the following requirements: Per 10 U.S.C.2109(c)(1), Practical Military Training, "A person who is not qualified for, and...will not be able to become qualified for, advanced training (POC),...shall not be permitted to participate in...practical military training," including Physical Fitness Training. All students must have a recent physical examination medically clearing individual to participate in class and proof of liability insurance. The syllabus will cover both cardiovascular and anaerobic activity, aimed at helping every participant to get in better shape regardless of fitness level. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: EPS
Title: MULTIVARIATE STATISTICS II
Description: This course is designed to deepen the students understanding and knowledge of interpreting and applying multivariate techniques to psychological research.  Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: MULTIVARIATE STATISTICS I
Description: This course provides students with a practical understanding of how to interpret and apply multivariate techniques to psychological research.  Letter grade only.

Subject: OTD
Title: EVID-BASED PRAC & PROF COMM
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: OTD 600, OTD 602, OTD 640, and OTD 651
Description: An introduction to the scholarly skills of locating, organizing and critically-appraising research literature related to practice is presented. An overview of professional communication style and dissemination skills is included. 2 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab. Letter grade only.

Subject: EDU
Title: INTRODUCTION TO SPECIAL ED
Description: This course introduces special education practices, policies, and philosophy, examining the role of teachers of children with special needs. The causes and characteristics of the most common conditions requiring accommodations for students with exceptionalities are examined, including special-needs children from non-majority racial, ethnic, or linguistic backgrounds.  Letter grade only.

Subject: STA
Title: INTRO MATH STATS II - HONORS
LongTitle: INTRODUCTION TO MATHEMATICAL STATISTICS II - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: STA 473 with grade of C or better and Honors Student Group
Description: Principles of statistical inference, estimation, and hypothesis testing. Letter grade only.

Subject: ACC
Title: AUDITING
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: ((ACC 300 or ISM 360) with a grade of C or better)
Pre- or Corequisite: ACC 356
Description: This course introduces audit and assurance services with a primary focus on the fundamental concepts of the audit process and applying those concepts in practical settings that require auditor judgment. The course emphasizes strategies and procedures employed by external auditors to gather audit evidence and report on a companys financial statements and internal controls over financial reporting. Letter grade only.

Subject: ACC
Title: GOV & NP ACCT
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: ((ACC 300 or ISM 360) with a grade of C or better)
Pre- or Corequisite: ACC 356
Description: Accounting theory, objectives, principles, and practice in the preparation, interpretation, and analysis of financial statements for state and local governments and nonprofit organizations. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: CREATIVE WRT: NONFICT - HONORS
LongTitle: SEMINAR IN CREATIVE WRITING: CREATIVE NONFICTION - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement, ENG 372W, and Honors Student Group
Description: Intensive study of the craft of creative nonfiction, emphasizing the writing of personal essays, memoirs, or subjective criticism. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit with different content.

Subject: ME
Title: RENEWABLE ENERGY
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: PHY 262 with a grade of C or better
ME majors must also have ME Fdtnl GPA Milestone
Description: Fundamentals concepts of renewable energy resources, conversion technology and hybrid system design with an emphasis towards solar photovoltaics and wind energy. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: HUMAN MICROBIOME ECOLOGY
Description: This course will cover the ecology of the microbial inhabitants of the human body, how the host depends on these microorganisms to maintain health, and consequences when these microbial communities are perturbed. The intent of this course is to introduce students to fundamental and novel concepts in human microbiome research, including original studies from many of the major contributors who have shaped the field. Co-convened with BIO 534. Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: GPU PROGRAMMING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 249 with a grade of C or better
Description: This course introduces students to general purpose computing on graphics processing units (GPGPU). Graphics processing units (GPUs) are parallel processors which typically contain thousands of low-powered cores. Therefore, the utility of GPUs has extended beyond graphics rendering, where they can be used for general purpose computation. This course will examine key concepts in GPU programming for general purpose computation. Co-convened with CS 553. Letter grade only.

Subject: CS
Title: SOFTWARE ENGINEERING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 249 with a grade of C or better
Description: Involves applying software engineering and software architecture principles and techniques in the construction of complex computer programs in a team setting. Individualized and team writing and oral presentation tasks. Ethical issues. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: TOPICS IN ESL
Description: Intensive study of a topic in ESL studies.  Letter grade only.  May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit with different content.

Subject: FRE
Title: FIRST YEAR FRENCH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: FRE 101 or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Continuation of FRE 101. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: PRO ETHICS, LEGAL STNDS & RES
LongTitle: PROFESSIONAL ETHICS, LEGAL STANDARDS AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admission to any Educational Psychology graduate program
Description: Current professional problems and ethics in the helping professions, including issues related to certification, licensure, confidentiality, forensic concerns, values, responsibilities, and professional and legal standards. Cross-listed with CPP 670. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: ADVANCED GLOBAL MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BBA 300, BBA 305W, and BBA Milestone
Description: This course provides an introduction to the cultural, legal, economic, and political environments of multinational firms.  Topics include managing in the global environment and strategic planning in a multicultural environment.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: BUSINESS RESEARCH DECISION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BBA 300, BBA 305W, and BBA Milestone
Description: Conceptual and applications of business research in decision making. Topics include decision-making process, problem definition, research methods and designs, data collection, sampling, and data analysis. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: CRITICAL THINKING & INNOVATION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BBA 300, BBA 305W, and BBA Milestone
Description: Examines the relationship between creativity, innovation, and critical thinking.  Topics include key concepts and techniques of creativity, innovation, application of skills in critical thinking, and decision making in small business administration.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: ENTREPR & SMALL BUSINESS MGT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BBA 300, BBA 305W, and BBA Milestone
Description: Concepts and techniques of organizing and managing small enterprises.  Emphasis on practical problems, especially the analysis of practical business case studies and interviews with local and regional businesses that will help the student identify the key steps in successfully managing an ongoing business for profitability and long term enterprise survival.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: PRINCIPLES OF MATHEMATICS II
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  MAT 150 with grade of C or better
Description: Mathematical principles and processes underlying mathematics instruction in grades K-8; geometry and measurement; statistics and probability. Letter grade only.

Subject: MUS
Title: VOCAL LITERATURE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MUS 241 or MUS 242) and Music Milestone (School of Music Proficiency Jury)
Description: Extensive study of the vocal literature. Co-convened with MUS 520. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: ADV EXER PHYSIOLOGY - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  BIO 338 and Honors Student Group
Description: Applies physiological principles to exercise circumstances; includes critical analysis of the effect of exercise on human physiologic function with in-depth examination of current literature.  This course is an option to satisfy the capstone requirement for exercise science majors.  Co-convenes with BIO 514.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: ADLT HLTH NRSNG PRACTICUM
Description: Required clinical practicum for Adult Health Nursing. Letter grade only.

Subject: ART
Title: HAND BUILDING
Description: Studio Course. Emphasizes skills in handbuilding for creation of sculpture or pottery.  May be repeated for a maximum of 6 units. Letter grade only.

Subject: ART
Title: CERAMICS II
Description: Studio course. Emphasizes increasing skill in wheel techniques and glaze experimentation; glazing and decoration techniques; kiln operations. May be repeated for a maximum of 6 units. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHI
Title: FIRST YEAR CHINESE (MANDARIN)
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CHI 101 or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Continuation of CHI 101. Significant lab drill work. Letter grade only.

Subject: AIS
Title: SOUNDING RESISTANCE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement with a grade of C or better
Description: An examination of the ways in which music has fueled American Indian resistance, from Red Power of the 1960s and 70s to the present. Letter grade only.

Subject: COM
Title: COMMUNICATION THEORY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement with a grade of C or better
Description: Introduces the nature, elements, concepts, and processes of communication in its many settings; examines vocabulary and research methods used in the study of communication. Letter grade only.

Subject: ACC
Title: ADVANCED BUSINESS LAW
Description: In-depth study of legal issues related to advising businesses from an accounting or senior management perspective, including contract law, securities law, administrative law, employment law, ethics and other legal topics.  Letter grade only.

Subject: GLG
Title: SEDIMENTOLOGY AND STRATIGRAPHY
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: GLG 309
Pre- or Corequisite: GLG 102
Description: Macroscopic study of sediments and sedimentary rocks, including origin, facies analysis, and basic stratigraphic principles and practices. 3 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab, plus field trips. Letter grade only.

Subject: LAN
Title: ISSUES IN SEC LANG AQSTN & EDU
LongTitle: ISSUES IN SECOND-LANGUAGE ACQUISITION AND EDUCATION
Description: Introductory survey of language acquisition, testing, and bilingualism.  Taught in English.  For teaching majors and minors only.  Letter grade only.

Subject: SPA
Title: SPANISH HEALTH PROF I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: SPA 102 or International Exchange Student Group
Description: This course provides foundational knowledge and skills for basic communication in Spanish within a health professions context. Through a combination of interactive activities, experiential learning, and targeted assessments, students will develop the four language skills (speaking, reading, listening, and writing), focusing on important linguistical and cultural aspects of medical Spanish. Letter grade only.

Subject: SPA
Title: SECOND YEAR SPANISH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: SPA 102 or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Continuation of SPA 101 and 102. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: BIOMECHANICS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 334, (MAT 125 or higher), and (PHY 111 or PHY 161)
Description: This course integrates anatomy, physiology and mechanics to explore the principles of human and animal movement. It highlights the diversity of techniques required to study the physics of movement. Co-convened with BIO 542. Letter grade only.

Subject: EPS
Title: SCHOOL PSYCHOLOGY PRACTICUM
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  EPS 606, EPS 670, EPS 673, EPS 674 and EPS 738
Description: Supervised administration and interpretation of tests applicable to the practice of school psychology; supervised experience with consultative, in-service, and direct interventions in educational settings. Contains a qualifying assessment for the internship. Pass-fail only. May be repeated for up to 8 units of credit.

Subject: MAT
Title: QUANTITATIVE REASONING
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 100 with a grade of P taken Fall 2013 or later) or (MAT 102X with a grade of C or better) or Math Placement Test Results (MTHPLACE 30+; ALEKS 30+; PLACE 43+) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Contemporary applications of quantitative methods involving number sense, ratios, proportions, rates, statistics, probability, graphical representations, and financial mathematics, with emphasis on application and understanding of these concepts in a wide range of contexts. Fulfills the Mathematics foundation requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: STA
Title: EXPL VISUAL DATA ANALYSIS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 100 with a grade of P taken Fall 2013 or later) or (MAT 102X with a grade of C or better) or Math Placement Test Results (MTHPLACE 30+; ALEKS 30+; PLACE 43+) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Graphical representation of data; categorical and continuous data. Bivariate and multivariate relationships; geographical and temporal considerations. Static and interactive graphics. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: PRINCIPLES OF MATHEMATICS I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 100 with a grade of P taken Fall 2013 or later) or (MAT 102X with a grade of C or better) or Math Placement Test Results (MTHPLACE 30+; ALEKS 30+; PLACE 43+) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Mathematical principles and processes underlying mathematics instruction in grades K-8; systems of whole numbers, integers, rational numbers, and irrationals. Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: FINANCIAL ACCT FOR BUSINESS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 100 with a grade of P taken Fall 2013 or later) or (MAT 102X with a grade of C or better) or Math Placement Test Results (MTHPLACE 30+; ALEKS 30+; PLACE 43+) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Introduces theory and practice in the preparation, analysis, and interpretation of general purpose financial statements for external users. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: BASIC PRINC IN PALLIATIVE CARE
Description: Provides an overview of core principles of palliative care. Emphasizes strategies to improve palliative care and the nurse as a member of an interdisciplinary team. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: GERONTOLOGY
Description: Explore beliefs, attitudes, stereotypes about the elderly. Includes theories of aging, normal aspects and changes, ethical issues, and successful aging through interdisciplinary health promotion and risk reduction strategies. Letter grade only.

Subject: ANT
Title: PROFESSIONAL DEV'T SEMINAR
Description: Designed to assist in preparing you for entry into your professional arena. Pass/Fail only.

Subject: MOL
Title: PRINCIPLES OF LEADERSHIP
Description: Introduction to general leadership theory, knowledge, and skills. Letter grade only.

Subject: MOL
Title: INTRO TO PROJECT MANAGEMENT
Description: Effective supervision of projects. Emphasis will be placed on specific skills and activities that promote efficient projects planning and execution and through control, coordination, and administration. Personnel management in terms of requisite supervisory skills is presented. Productivity measurement and improvement are introduced. Letter grade only.

Subject: MOL
Title: DIVERSITY, CONFLICT & COMMUNIC
Description: Innovation in organizations though engagement of diverse cultures and the effective conflict negotiation and communication skill sets that make it possible. Letter grade only.

Subject: MOL
Title: CONTEMPORARY DEVELOPMENTS
Description: Examines recent trends and investigations in a selected area of a particular field of study.  May be offered no more than three times before being submitted for a permanent course number.  May be repeated for credit.  Pass-fail or letter grade.

Subject: SOC
Title: INTERNSHIP
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Sociology major or minor and junior status or higher
Description: Application of theory, skills, and knowledge of sociology in direct public or private human service settings. Department consent required. Pass-fail only. May be repeated for up to 12 units of credit.

Subject: ECI
Title: STUDENT TEACHING & INTERNSHIP
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to Elem Ed-Certification (MEd). If a teacher candidate plans to student teach in Middle School, a passing score on the appropriate Arizona Educator Subject Matter Exam.
Description: This field experience consists of directed teaching experience in an elementary school with directed and cooperative preparation, teaching and evaluation. Pass/Fail only.  May be repeated for up to 11 units of credit.

Subject: CCS
Title: CCS CAPSTONE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CCS 350W and (Junior status or higher)
Description: A topics-oriented, interdisciplinary seminar in which students pursue a project in their emphasis; includes comparative analysis of artistic, literary, and religious practices from the disciplinary perspectives in CCS. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 6 units of credit.

Subject: ISM
Title: HARDWARE AND OPERATING SYSTEMS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ISM 220 with a grade of C or better and Business Professional Program Milestone
Description: Operating system fundamentals including installation, configuration, administration, use, and analysis of leading operating systems. Coverage will include extensive practical work to include use of command language scripting, file systems, security, and user rights. Letter grade only. May be repeated for up to 9 units of credit.

Subject: BA
Title: BUSINESS CONSULTING SKILLS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Admission to the MBA Program, BA 587, BA 650, and BA 653  Corequisite:  BA 642 and BA 654
Description: This course provides an overview of management, marketing, and communication skills necessary for success for MBA Consulting Concentration graduates.  These skills contribute to consulting and management competencies for success in competitive organizations.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NAU
Title: STUDY SKILLS & COLLEGE SUCCESS
LongTitle: TOPICS IN STUDY SKILLS AND COLLEGE SUCCESS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  Freshmen status or cumulative GPA less than 2.00
Description: This course supports first-year students as they transition into a higher education community.  Promoting the principles of Self-Regulated Learning, this course empowers students by focusing on effective study strategies, campus resources, campus culture, and foundational academic skills, as well as skills linked with both personal and social wellness through a theme related to the first-year.  Letter grade only.  Prerequisite:  Freshmen status with an un-weighted core high school GPA of 2.75 or below.

Subject: BIO
Title: DATA ANALYSIS & REP
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: STA 270 and Foundation English Requirement
Description: This course emphasizes quantitative approaches, graphical representation, and science writing, and prepares students for the presentation of independent field research in a public and professional forum. Letter grade only.

Subject: AIS
Title: INDIGENIZING RESEARCH - HONORS
LongTitle: INDIGENIZING RESEARCH METHODS AND ISSUES - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  One 200- or 300-level AIS-Applied Indigenous Studies course, Honors Student Group
Description: The course introduces students to the ideological, political, and practical issues involved in managing and conducting research with indigenous communities. Letter grade only.

Subject: PSY
Title: ADV RSCH MTHDS IN PSY
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: PSY 202; (PSY 230 or PSY 231); (PSY 290 or PSY 302W); and Foundation English Requirement
Description: This course will further your understanding of research principles in psychological science that were introduced in PSY 290 (Research Methods in Psychology), with an emphasis on learning about, evaluating, and applying the methods and techniques used to conduct and report psychological research. You will review basic research methods before covering advanced topics in research design, methodology, measurement, and statistical analysis. Goals of this course include: (1) honing written communication skills, and (2) refining scientific inquiry and critical thinking skills in the field of psychology. This junior-level writing course is aligned with the second (Scientific Inquiry and Critical Thinking) and fourth (Communication) learning goals of the BS in Psychological Sciences. This course emphasizes the essential skill of effective writing through its principal assignment, a group research project that results in a professional poster and oral presentation, and individually written APA

Subject: FW
Title: EXRCSE PROG DES & MOD - HONORS
LongTitle: EXERCISE PROGRAM DESIGN AND MODIFICATION - HONORS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: (HS 200, BIO 192, BIO 201, BIO 202, FW 325, FW 330, and NTS 135); and Honors Student Group
Pre- or Corequisite: FW 380

Prerequisites must have grades of C or better
Description: This capstone course serves as a culminating experience for students in which they synthesize and apply the knowledge and skills to design and implement comprehensive exercise programs for apparently healthy individuals and those with health challenges who are able to exercise independently to enhance quality of life, improve health-related physical fitness, performance, and promote lasting health behavior change. Letter grade only.

Subject: MUP
Title: INSTRUMENTAL CONDUCTING
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: MUS 431 
Pre- or corequisite: MUS 242
All prerequisites must have a grade of C or better
Description: Techniques, organization, materials, and interpretation for large instrumental groups.  Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: MATH CAPSTONE EXPERIENCE
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: MAT 320 and (MAT 411, MAT 431, or STA 473); Senior status; and Admission to Mathematics BS program
All prerequisites must have grades of C or better
Description: Students will work with faculty to identify a topic area, work with other students in the same area to learn supporting material and complete an independent project. The project will culminate in an oral presentation and a written report. This course should be taken within two semesters of the students intended graduation date. Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: INSTRUMENTAL ANALYSIS LAB
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: CHM 320L
Pre- or Corequisite: CHM 425C
Description: Hands-on overview of modern chemical instrumentation: how instruments work and applications to chemical problems.  Provides experience with instruments based on optical spectroscopy and chromatography/mass spectrometry. This course in conjunction with CHM 425C satisfies NAUs capstone requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: TCHING CLDE STUDENTS
LongTitle: TEACHING CULTURALLY AND LINGUISTICALLY  DIVERSE STUDENTS WITH EXCEPTIONALITIES
Description: Unique contributions of culture and language to educational performance and the educational interventions designed to maximize each childs individual potential.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: INCL COLLAB CLDE
LongTitle: INCL COLLABORATIVE MTHDS FOR TCHING CULTURALLY & LINGUISTICALLY DIVERSE STUDENTS W/ EXCEPTIONALITIE
Description: Philosophy, rationale, methods, and materials for teaching culturally linguistically diverse students with exceptionalities in inclusive settings utilizing team teaching, parallel teaching, co-teaching, and consultative teaching.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: ASSMT CLDE STUDENTS
LongTitle: LIMITING BIAS IN ASSESSMENT OF CULTURALLY AND LINGUISTICALLY DIVERSE STUDENTS WITH EXCEPTIONALITIES
Description: Pre-referral procedures, educational and psychological assessment techniques and instruments, educational diagnosis, and placement procedures.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BBA
Title: STRATEGIC LOGISTICS
LongTitle: STRATEGIC LOGISTICS AND SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT
Description: Covers the formulation and implementation of logistics strategy for competitive advantage, including analysis of logistics capabilities, enhancement of strategy-supportive capabilities, and integration with strategically chosen supply chain partners. Letter grade only.

Subject: HS
Title: MATERNAL CHILD & SEXUAL HEALTH
Prerequisites: Prerequisite:  HS 200 with a grade of C or better
Description: Introduces major issues and trends that affect the health and well-being of women, infants, children, and adolescents in domestic and international settings.  Examines healthy relationships, contraception, and STI prevention.  Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: FAMLY PRMRY HLTH CARE PRAC III
LongTitle: FAMILY PRIMARY HEALTH CARE PRACTICUM III
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: NUR 662 and NUR 663 
Corequisite: NUR 664
Description: This as the final primary care course emphasizes crisis intervention, chronic illness, and complex health conditions for the family unit across the life span.  Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: GENERAL PATHOLOGY
Description: Surveys disease processes affecting body systems; cell death and inflammation; emphasizes altered physiological mechanisms in cells and organ systems.  Letter grade only.

Subject: CHM
Title: FUNDMTL ORGANIC CHEM  RECITATN
Description: Optional recitation section covering problem-solving techniques in organic chemistry.  Pass-fail only.

Subject: HS
Title: METHODS FOR COMM HLTH PROMO
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: HS 200 and HS 205 with grades of C or better
Corequisite: HS 300 and HS 305
Description: A skills-based approach to the various methods and strategies required to conduct comprehensive health promotion programs. Letter grade only.

Subject: BIO
Title: MOLECULAR ECOLOGY & EVOLUTION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: BIO 181, BIO 182, BIO 226, and BIO 244
Description: This course explores the molecular genetic techniques currently used in behavioral, ecological, and evolutionary research. Techniques covered include a broad spectrum of methods including PCR, DNA sequencing (both Sanger and Next Generation) and genetic fingerprinting methods such as AFLPs and MSATs. The molecular basis for each method/technique is covered, as are appropriate methods of data analysis with specific applications to problems in organismal biology and evolutionary ecology. Co-convened with BIO 519. Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: SOCIOLOGY OF CULTURE
Description: This class explores theoretical approaches to the sociological understanding of culture.  Theories are applied to a variety of cultural forms, such as film, music, and art, and students learn how to access and analyze various forms of cultural data in terms of race, ethnicity, class, gender, sexuality and ability.  Letter grade only.

Subject: SOC
Title: HUMAN REPRODUCTION IN SOCIETY
Description: The process of human reproduction is social as well as biological, linking gendered bodies to social structure and institutions. In this course, students are introduced to contemporary issues of human reproduction in society. We will explore how culture and social structure shape reproductive decisions, attitudes, practices, and identities for individuals and communities. With a particular focus on the intersecting identities of gender, race, class, sexuality, and disability, we will examine sociological research on a wide range of topics, such as contraception, abortion, reproductive technologies, childbirth, adoption, parenthood, infertility, and the social construction of reproductive norms, values, and beliefs. Letter grade only.

Subject: ACC
Title: GOV & NP ACCT - HONORS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: ((ACC 300 or ISM 360) with a grade of C or better); Honors Student Group
Pre- or Corequisite: ACC 356
Description: Accounting theory, objectives, principles, and practice in the preparation, interpretation, and analysis of financial statements for state and local governments and nonprofit organizations. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: FNDMTLS NUR PRACT CLINICAL
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: Admission to Traditional or Compressed BSN
Corequisite:
TBSN: (NUR 322, NUR 323, NUR 326, NUR 327, NUR 330, and NUR 355)
CBSN: (NUR 322, NUR 323, NUR 326, NUR 327, NUR 330, NUR 352, and NUR 355)
Description: Building on co-requisite fundamentals courses, this is a required clinical practicum for development and practice of basic nursing skills including the psychomotor skill development needed to implement safe and effective patient centered care. Emphasis is placed on basic nursing skills, completing health histories and physical assessments, and recognizing expected findings in patients in the clinical setting, usually a long-term care facility. Upon completion of this course, students will have mastered a foundational understanding of the nursing process, basic nursing skills, basic patient assessment, beginning medication administration, a beginning understanding of global health, and an introduction to body systems and functions. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: NURSING CARE OF FAMILIES
Description: Nursing care of families experiencing complex health transitions within community settings. Letter grade only.

Subject: CENE
Title: WATER RESOURCES I - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: CENE 251 and MAT 238 with grades of C or better; Honors Student Group
Description: Introduction to the fundamentals of hydraulic analysis, including the theory, concepts and use of conservation laws as applied to hydrostatics, pipe flow, pipe networks, turbo-machinery, hydraulic thrust forces and open-channel flow. Letter grade only.

Subject: ISM
Title: CAPSTONE EXPERIENCE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ISM 530, ISM 555, ISM 560, and ISM 590
Description: In this capstone course, students are required to define a Management Information Systems (MIS) problem and devise solutions using their acquired knowledge and skills. Emphasis is also placed on critical thinking, communication, teamwork, presentation, and writing skills. Collaborative efforts with industry partners or faculty advisors to formulate projects spanning various MIS facets are encouraged. The course concludes with a final presentation, where teams present their work to a broader audience including their industry partners or faculty advisors for evaluation. A detailed written report will also be submitted following the presentation. Letter grade only.

Subject: NUR
Title: DEVELOPMENTAL TRANSITIONS
LongTitle: DEVELOPMENTAL AND HEALTH-ILLNESS TRANSITIONS OF CHILD-BEARING AND CHILD-REARING FAMILIES
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite:  NUR 347, NUR 347L, NUR 349, NUR 349L  
Corequisite:  NUR 411L, NUR 440
Description: Focuses on nursing care for obstetric and pediatric patients.  Letter grade only.

Subject: PHY
Title: UNIV PHYSICS II FOR PHYSICISTS
Prerequisites:
Prerequisite: MAT 136 and PHY 171
Corequisite: MAT 137
Description: The second semester of a three-semester, calculus-based, introductory physics sequence especially designed for physics and astronomy majors.  This course covers all topics in PHY 262 including electric fields, magnetic fields, Maxwells equations, and the First and Second Laws of Thermodynamics, and goes beyond PHY262 by adding topics that are especially useful in preparation for upper division physics courses including time-varying electric fields, time varying-magnetic fields, electromagnetic waves, and entropy.  4 hrs. lecture, 3 hrs. lab.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ECO
Title: INTRO TO ECONOMETRICS - HONORS
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: ECO 201, ECO 284, ECO 285, ECO 321; Senior status or higher; and Honors Student Group
Description: This course is an examination of the theory and application of econometrics. The focus is on using the basic regression model, understanding Ordinary Least Squares, analyzing the assumptions of the Classical Model. The course will focus on violations of the assumptions of the Classical Model, with particular focus on practical remedies for these violations and applications of regression analysis in areas such as cross-sectional  methods, simultaneous equations, dummy dependent variable techniques, and time-series analysis. Letter grade only.

Subject: HA
Title: CLUB MANAGEMENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Hotel & Restaurant Mgt (BS), or Hotel & Restaurant Mgt Minor, or Business Hospitality (UCERT)
Description: Introduces the history, organization, management, and recreation of various private country and city clubs. Letter grade only.

Subject: CENE
Title: PRESTRESSED CONCRETE DESIGN
Description: Principles in the analysis and design of prestressed and post-tensioned concrete elements. Service and strength limit state analysis and design, detailing of components, calculation of losses for prestressed and post-tensioned members. Letter grade only.

Subject: CSD
Title: SPEECH SCIENCE
Description: This course will provide a basic understanding of the processes involved in normal speech production and perception. Content will include the physics of sound, sound spectrography, the physiological bases of respiration, voice production, and resonance of the vocal tract, acoustic and physiological phonetics, perception of consonants and vowels, and the neural control of speech. Letter grade only.

Subject: ESE
Title: ADV MTHDS EC SP ED 0 TO 5
LongTitle: ADVANCED METHODS IN EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION FOR CHILDREN BIRTH TO FIVE
Description: Evidence-based methods of supporting and assisting children with disabilities ages birth to five and their families. Co-convened with ESE 456. Letter grade only.

Subject: MAT
Title: CALCULUS I
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: (MAT 125 with a grade of C or better) or Math Placement Test Results (MTHPLACE 65+; ALEKS 65+; PLACE 70+) or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Calculus of one variable; basic concepts, interpretations, techniques, and applications of differentiation and integration. For the Liberal Studies Program, this course will fulfill either the Mathematics foundation requirement or a Science Applied Science distribution block requirement. If you have already fulfilled your Mathematics foundation requirement, this course will fulfill a Science Applied Science distribution block requirement. For the General Studies Program, this course will fulfill either the Mathematics foundation requirement or a Natural Science distribution block requirement. If you have already fulfilled your Mathematics foundation requirement, this course will fulfill a Natural Science distribution block requirement. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: INTRO CREATIVE WRTG:  FICTION
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Beginning course in short-story writing that emphasizes the composition and revision of student stories. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: ENGLISH GRAMMAR AND USAGE
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Current views on American English usages. Surveys prescriptive-descriptive grammar debate in relation to norms, dialects, and cultural values. Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: AMER LIT FRM 1865 TO PRESENT
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Major authors and movements in the literature of the United States from 1865 to the present.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
Title: AMER LIT FRM COLONIAL TO 1865
LongTitle: AMERICAN LITERATURE FROM COLONIAL TIMES TO 1865
Prerequisites: Prerequisite: Foundation English Requirement or International Exchange Student Group
Description: Major authors and movements in the literature of the United States from colonial times to 1865.  Letter grade only.

Subject: ENG
